The importability of rare species is carefully controlled by international treaties. 稀有物種的可輸入性受國際條約的嚴格控制。
The goods were declared importable after passing inspection at the border. 貨物在邊境通過檢查後被宣布為可進口的。
Not all agricultural products are importable due to phytosanitary rules. 並非所有農產品都可以進口，因為有植物檢疫規定。
The importableness of the product was questioned by customs officials. 海關官員對該產品的可進口性提出了質疑。
The importableness of certain chemicals is subject to strict regulation. 某些化學品的可進口性受嚴格監管。

meaning: 以可進口的方式；允許進口地
sentences: The goods were classified importably under the current trade agreement. 根據現行貿易協定，這些商品被歸類為可進口的。
The exemption allowed the equipment to enter importably without duties. 豁免允許設備以可進口的方式免稅進入。
The teacher emphasized the importance of regular practice. 老師強調了定期練習的重要性。
Education is of great importance for a successful future. 教育對於成功的未來至關重要。

meaning: 重要性；重大意義（古體詞）
sentences: The importancy of the decision was not lost on any of the ministers. 這個決定的重要性對任何一位部長來說都是顯而易見的。
He stressed the importancy of acting quickly in the emergency. 他強調了在緊急情況下迅速行動的重要性。
It is important to get enough sleep for your health. 為了健康，獲得足夠的睡眠很重要。
Please keep these important documents in a safe place. 請將這些重要的文件放在安全的地方。

meaning: 用於將語句標記為具有重要性。
sentences: If someone acts importantly, they do so in a way that is used to mark a statement as having importance. 如果某人的行為很重要，那麼他們這樣做的方式就是將某句話標記為具有重要性。
The speaker responded importantly during the discussion. 演講者在討論中做出了重要回應。

meaning: 進口的；從國外引進一國的商品或貨物。
sentences: The term importation refers to that which is imported; commodities or wares introduced into a country from abroad. 進口一詞是指進口的；從國外引進一國的商品或貨物。
The lesson introduced importation as a useful term to understand. 本課程介紹了導入作為一個需要理解的有用術語。

meaning: 進口者或進口者：尤指將貨物進口到某個國家的個人或公司。
sentences: India is the world's biggest importer of gold. 印度是世界上最大的黃金進口國。
The data importer has crashed. Did we receive a corrupted file? 資料導入器已崩潰。我們收到了損壞的文件嗎？

meaning: 不重要的；無意義的（古體詞）
sentences: The importless details were cut from the report to save time. 無意義的細節從報告中被刪除以節省時間。
He dismissed the complaint as importless and moved on. 他將投訴視為不重要的並繼續前進。
The importment of the treaty was debated by scholars for decades. 這項條約的重要性被學者爭論了幾十年。
The message bore great importment to the future of the empire. 這條信息對帝國的未來具有重大意義。

meaning: 以肖像描繪；以圖像呈現（極罕見詞）
sentences: The artist sought to importraiture the essence of her subject beyond mere likeness. 藝術家試圖通過肖像描繪超越單純相似性地呈現主題的本質。
Medieval painters would importraiture saints according to their canonical attributes. 中世紀畫家會根據其規範屬性以肖像描繪聖人。

meaning: 描繪；以圖像呈現（古體詞）
sentences: The tapestry sought to importray the great battles of the kingdom's history. 掛毯試圖描繪王國歷史上的偉大戰役。
Drama can importray complex human emotions more vividly than prose. 戲劇可以比散文更生動地描繪複雜的人類情感。

meaning: 糾纏；強求；不斷要求（古體詞）
sentences: His importunacy in asking for a raise eventually annoyed his employer. 他不斷要求加薪的行為最終惹惱了他的雇主。
The petitioner's importunacy finally wore down the official's resistance. 請願者的糾纏最終磨損了官員的抵抗力。
The importunance of the creditors made daily life difficult for the debtor. 債權人的糾纏使債務人的日常生活變得困難。
Constant importunance is an unpleasant trait that drives people away. 不斷的糾纏是一種令人不愉快的特質，會驅走他人。

meaning: 糾纏不休的；強求的；迫切的
sentences: The importunate beggar blocked the entrance, pestering every passerby. 糾纏不休的乞丐堵住了入口，纏擾每一個路人。
He was importunate in his requests, repeating them until they were granted. 他在要求方面是糾纏不休的，不斷重複直到得到滿足。
She importunately pestered the manager until he agreed to a refund. 她糾纏地騷擾經理，直到他同意退款。
The salesperson importunately followed the customer around the store. 推銷員糾纏地跟著顧客在商店裡轉來轉去。
The importunateness of the child trying to get a toy was exhausting. 孩子試圖得到一個玩具的糾纏不休令人精疲力竭。
His importunateness in negotiations was a known tactical approach. 他在談判中的糾纏不休是一種眾所周知的戰術方法。
The importunator would not leave until he had received an answer. 糾纏者不得到回答就不走。
As an importunator, she gained the reputation of being difficult to deal with. 作為糾纏者，她獲得了難以相處的聲譽。
Tourists are sometimes importuned by street vendors selling cheap souvenirs. 遊客有時被出售廉價紀念品的街頭小販糾纏。
She importuned the committee with repeated requests for funding. 她用一再要求資助的方式纏擾委員會。
He importunely followed up his request every single day. 他每天都糾纏地追蹤他的請求。
She importunely sought approval from everyone she met. 她糾纏地向她遇到的每個人尋求認可。

meaning: 糾纏；強求（importuning 的名詞形式）
sentences: The constant importunement from clients left the lawyer drained. 客戶的不斷糾纏讓律師感到精疲力竭。
His importunement of the administration finally led to a policy review. 他對行政機構的糾纏最終導致了政策審查。
The persistent importuner was asked to leave the premises. 這位持續糾纏的人被要求離開場所。
As an importuner, he was well known for wearing down people with requests. 作為一個糾纏者，他以不斷用請求磨損他人而聞名。
The creditor's importunity forced the debtor to seek legal protection. 債權人的糾纏迫使債務人尋求法律保護。
His importunity in seeking promotions made him unpopular at work. 他在尋求晉升方面的糾纏使他在工作中不受歡迎。
The fine was deemed imposable under current regulations. 根據現行法規，罰款被認為是可施加的。
The court ruled that the condition was imposable on the defendant. 法院裁定該條件可施加於被告。

meaning: 可施加性；可強加性（罕見詞）
sentences: The imposableness of the tax was debated in parliament. 稅款的可施加性在議會中被辯論。
Lawyers questioned the imposableness of the new conditions on existing contracts. 律師們質疑新條件對現有合同的可施加性。
The imposal of heavy duties on foreign goods affected trade significantly. 對外國商品徵收重稅的施加對貿易產生了重大影響。
The imposal of martial law was met with protests across the country. 戒嚴令的施加在全國各地遭到了抗議。
The government decided to impose a new tax on luxury goods now. 政府決定現在對奢侈品徵收（強加）一項新稅。
Don't try to impose your personal values and beliefs on others. 不要試圖將妳個人的價值觀與信念強加於他人。
The imposement of strict curfews was intended to restore order. 嚴格宵禁令的施加旨在恢復秩序。
The imposement of new regulations on the industry required significant adjustments. 對行業施加新法規需要重大調整。
As the main imposer of the rules, the chairman had significant authority. 作為規則的主要施加者，主席擁有重要的權威。
The imposer of unjust laws eventually faced public revolt. 不公平法律的施加者最終面臨公眾的反抗。
The new library is an imposing building made of glass and steel. 新圖書館是一座由玻璃和鋼鐵構成的壯觀建築。
He has an imposing presence that commands respect from everyone. 他有一種令人印象深刻的風度（氣場），贏得了每個人的尊重。
The cathedral rose imposingly above the city skyline. 大教堂壯觀地聳立於城市天際線之上。
He walked imposingly into the room, commanding everyone's attention. 他威嚴地走進房間，吸引了所有人的注意。
The imposingness of the mountain range left the travelers speechless. 山脈的壯觀使旅行者們無言以對。
The imposingness of the judge's presence silenced the courtroom. 法官存在的威嚴使法庭靜默下來。

meaning: 在宗教儀式中按手在人身上的做法；使用例如在確認和任命中。
sentences: The term imposition refers to a practice of laying hands on a person in a religious ceremony; used e.g. in confirmation and ordination. 強加一詞是指在宗教儀式中按手在人身上的做法；使用例如在確認和任命中。
The lesson introduced imposition as a useful term to understand. 本課程介紹了強加作為一個需要理解的有用術語。

meaning: 強制性的；施加性的（罕見詞）
sentences: The impositional nature of the directive angered many citizens. 指令的強制性性質激怒了許多公民。
Impositional policies rarely achieve lasting compliance without enforcement. 強制性政策在沒有執法的情況下很少能實現持久的合規。
The impositive regulations left little room for individual choice. 強加的法規幾乎沒有給個人選擇留下空間。
The emperor's impositive decrees were obeyed without question. 皇帝的強加法令被毫無疑問地服從。

meaning: 使不可能；宣稱不可能的行為（罕見詞）
sentences: The impossibilification of compromise by both sides prolonged the conflict. 雙方對妥協的宣稱不可能延長了衝突。
Bureaucratic impossibilification of simple processes frustrated applicants. 官僚主義對簡單程序的宣稱不可能使申請者感到沮喪。

meaning: 不可能主義（認為改革不可能的政治立場）
sentences: Impossibilism is a political tendency that rejects gradual reform as insufficient. 不可能主義是一種政治傾向，拒絕漸進改革，認為其不夠充分。
Critics accused the faction of practicing impossibilism rather than constructive politics. 批評者指責該派別實行不可能主義而非建設性政治。

meaning: 不可能主義者；認為改革不可能的人
sentences: As an impossibilist, he rejected all incremental reforms as useless concessions. 作為不可能主義者，他拒絕所有漸進式改革，認為它們是無用的讓步。
The impossibilist faction split from the party over strategic disagreements. 不可能主義者派別因戰略分歧而從黨分裂出去。

meaning: 使成為不可能；阻止（古體詞）
sentences: The lack of funding threatened to impossibilitate the entire research program. 資金匱乏威脅使整個研究計劃成為不可能。
Technical failures served to impossibilitate the launch of the satellite. 技術故障導致衛星的發射成為不可能。
Meeting the deadline is an impossibility; there is no way we can be ready in time. 在最後期限前完成任務是不可能的；我們不可能及時做好準備。
The term impossibility refers to Something that is impossible. 「不可能」一詞指的是不可能的事。
It's impossible to finish the work in such a short time. 在這麼短的時間內完成工作是不可能的。
He thought the task was impossible, but he managed to do it. 他以為這項任務是不可能的，但他還是設法完成了。
The impossibleness of the task was acknowledged by everyone involved. 所有參與者都承認了這項任務的不可能性。
He insisted on the impossibleness of completing the project on time. 他堅持認為按時完成項目是不可能的。
The topology problem was impossibly difficult. 拓樸問題極為困難。
The team responded impossibly to the change. 團隊對這項變化做出了不可思議的反應。
A heavy impost on imported goods made domestic products more competitive. 對進口商品徵收的重稅使國內產品更具競爭力。
The impost of the arch was decorated with intricate carvings. 拱門的柱頭裝飾著精緻的雕刻。

meaning: 冒名頂替者；騙子（impostor 的變體拼寫）
sentences: The imposter claimed to be a doctor but had no medical qualifications. 這位冒名頂替者聲稱是醫生，但沒有醫學資格。
The classic card game 'Among Us' is inspired by the concept of an imposter among allies. 經典卡牌遊戲《Among Us》的靈感來自於同伴中有冒名頂替者的概念。

meaning: 欺詐性的；冒名頂替的（罕見詞）
sentences: The imposterous claims of the faith healer were quickly exposed. 這位信仰治療師的欺詐性聲明很快被揭露。
His imposterous behavior was eventually discovered by the authorities. 他的欺詐性行為最終被當局發現。
The impostor passed himself off as a government official for years. 冒名頂替者多年來以政府官員的身份自居。
The detective uncovered the impostor through careful investigation. 偵探通過仔細調查揭穿了冒名頂替者。

meaning: 冒名頂替行為；詐騙主義
sentences: Impostorism in the medical profession endangers patients' lives. 醫療行業的冒名頂替行為危及患者的生命。
The era was marked by widespread impostorism in financial markets. 那個時代的特點是金融市場上廣泛的詐騙主義。

meaning: 冒名頂替者的身份或行為
sentences: His impostorship was revealed when his false credentials were exposed. 當他的虛假證書被揭露時，他的冒名頂替者身份被曝光。
Impostorship in academia destroys the credibility of legitimate research. 學術界的冒名頂替行為破壞了合法研究的可信度。

meaning: 女性冒名頂替者；女性騙子
sentences: The impostress had convinced an entire town she was a famous actress. 這位女性冒名頂替者說服了整個小鎮，讓他們相信她是一位著名女演員。
The novel's protagonist is a brilliant impostress who outwits her adversaries. 小說的主角是一位才華橫溢的女性冒名頂替者，她比對手更聰明。

meaning: 女性冒名頂替者（拉丁語陰性形式）
sentences: The historical case of the impostrix who claimed to be a lost princess became a famous story. 聲稱自己是失蹤公主的女性冒名頂替者的歷史案例成為了一個著名故事。
As an impostrix, she had successfully assumed multiple false identities. 作為一名女性冒名頂替者，她成功地假扮了多個虛假身份。

meaning: 欺詐性的；冒名頂替的（罕見詞）
sentences: The impostrous scheme was eventually uncovered by a diligent investigator. 這個欺詐性計劃最終被一位勤奮的調查員揭露。
His impostrous claims fooled many investors before the fraud was reported. 在欺詐行為被舉報之前，他的欺詐性聲明欺騙了許多投資者。
The wound began to impostumate due to infection, requiring medical attention. 傷口因感染開始化膿，需要醫療處理。
Medieval physicians feared wounds that would impostumate under bandages. 中世紀的醫生害怕繃帶下化膿的傷口。

meaning: 膿腫的形成；化膿（古體詞）
sentences: Impostumation of the infected tissue required lancing to drain the pus. 感染組織的化膿需要切開引流膿液。
Ancient medical texts described impostumation as a dangerous complication of wounds. 古代醫學文本將化膿描述為傷口的危險並發症。
Shakespeare mentioned impostume in Hamlet to suggest moral corruption. 莎士比亞在《哈姆雷特》中提到膿腫，暗示道德腐敗。
The physician lanced the impostume to relieve the patient's pain. 醫生切開膿腫以緩解患者的疼痛。
The scam artist's imposture was uncovered by a sharp-eyed journalist. 這位詐騙藝術家的欺騙被一位眼尖的記者揭穿了。
The imposture lasted for years before anyone questioned her credentials. 這場欺騙持續了幾年，才有人質疑她的資格。

meaning: 欺騙主義；詐騙行為的傾向
sentences: Imposturism in online communities has grown with the rise of anonymous accounts. 隨著匿名帳戶的興起，線上社區中的欺騙主義有所增長。
The culture of imposturism in the company led to its eventual collapse. 公司文化中的欺騙主義最終導致了公司的崩潰。
The imposturous character of the scheme was evident from the beginning. 這個計劃的欺詐性性質從一開始就顯而易見。
His imposturous claims about his credentials deceived many institutions. 他關於自己資格的欺詐性聲明欺騙了許多機構。

meaning: 欺騙；冒名頂替（imposture 的古體）
sentences: The imposure of the false priest was discovered during a holy ceremony. 這位假牧師的欺騙在一次神聖儀式期間被發現。
Exposing imposure in the scientific community is essential for integrity. 揭露科學界的欺騙對誠信至關重要。

meaning: 稅（法語借詞，也指懲罰性抄寫作業）
sentences: The impot was a heavy burden on the peasantry of medieval France. 稅是中世紀法國農民的沉重負擔。
At the old British boarding school, an impot was extra writing assigned as punishment. 在古老的英國寄宿學校，懲罰性抄寫作業是作為懲罰布置的額外書寫。

meaning: 不可飲用的；無法飲用的
sentences: The river water was rendered impotable by industrial pollution. 河水因工業污染而變得不可飲用。
Travelers must check whether the local tap water is impotable before drinking. 旅行者在飲用之前必須檢查當地自來水是否不可飲用。
The government's impotence in resolving the crisis drew widespread criticism. 政府在解決危機方面的無能為力引發了廣泛批評。
Medical treatments for impotence have improved greatly in recent decades. 近幾十年來，陽痿的醫療治療有了很大改善。

meaning: 無能為力；缺乏力量（impotence 的變體）
sentences: The political impotency of the minority party frustrated its supporters. 少數黨的政治無能讓其支持者感到沮喪。
His impotency in the face of the crisis was evident to all observers. 他在危機面前的無能為力對所有觀察者來說都是顯而易見的。

meaning: 無能為力的；軟弱的；（醫學上）陽痿的
sentences: The impotent rage he felt turned inward, causing despair. 他感受到的無能為力的憤怒轉向了內部，引發了絕望。
Without sufficient authority, the committee was impotent to enforce change. 沒有足夠的權力，委員會無能為力地執行變革。
He stood impotently by as the situation worsened beyond his control. 當情況惡化到超出他的控制時，他無能為力地站在一旁。
The small nation could only impotently protest the superpower's actions. 這個小國只能無能為力地抗議超級大國的行動。
His impotentness in the situation left him deeply frustrated. 他在這種情況下的無能為力使他深感沮喪。
The impotentness of the relief effort in the face of the disaster was tragic. 賑災工作面對災難的無能為力是悲劇性的。

meaning: 扣押；拘留；收押（動物、車輛等）
sentences: The police impounded the illegally parked vehicles overnight. 警察在夜間扣押了違規停放的車輛。
The court ordered the authorities to impound all assets of the accused. 法院命令當局扣押被告的所有資產。

meaning: 可被扣押的；可被拘押的
sentences: Vehicles parked in restricted zones are impoundable by law. 依法，停放在限制區域的車輛可被扣押。
The judge declared the assets impoundable until the verdict was reached. 法官宣布在裁決達成之前資產可被扣押。
The impoundage fee for the towed car was higher than expected. 被拖走的汽車的扣押費用高於預期。
The impoundage of stray animals was carried out by animal control officers. 流浪動物的扣押由動物管控官員執行。

meaning: 扣押者；執行扣押的人或機構
sentences: The impounder labeled each item carefully before placing it in storage. 扣押者在將每件物品放入存儲之前仔細標記。
As the legal impounder, the bank had the right to seize the debtor's property. 作為合法的扣押者，銀行有權沒收債務人的財產。
The impoundment of the river waters created a massive reservoir. 對河流水域的蓄水創造了一個巨大的水庫。
Vehicle impoundment serves as both a penalty and a deterrent. 車輛扣押既是懲罰也是威懾手段。
Wars tend to impoverish nations and set back development by decades. 戰爭往往使國家陷入貧窮，使發展倒退幾十年。
Overfarming can impoverish the soil and reduce its fertility. 過度農耕可能耗盡土壤，降低其肥沃度。
The charity provides food and aid to impoverished families. 該慈善機構為貧困家庭提供食物和援助。
The soil has become impoverished due to over-farming. 由於過度耕種，土壤已經變得貧瘠。

meaning: 使人貧窮的事物或人；剝削者
sentences: Exploitative landlords were seen as impoverishers of the rural poor. 剝削性地主被視為農村窮人的掠奪者。
The economic crisis was identified as the primary impoverisher of the middle class. 經濟危機被確定為中產階級的主要貧困化根源。
The impoverishment of the rural population was a major social problem. 農村人口的貧困化是一個主要的社會問題。
The impoverishment of the soil over years of monoculture reduced crop yields. 多年單一種植使土壤貧乏，降低了農作物產量。
The impracticability of the plan was evident from the start. 這個計劃的不可行性從一開始就顯而易見。
Engineers cited the impracticability of the proposed design due to structural limitations. 工程師們引用了由於結構限制，所提議設計的不可行性。
The proposed route was impracticable during winter months due to heavy snow. 由於大雪，所提議的路線在冬季幾個月是不可行的。
His idea was brilliant but ultimately impracticable given the budget constraints. 他的想法很出色，但鑑於預算限制，最終是不切實際的。
The impracticableness of the proposal was quickly recognized by the committee. 該提案的不可行性很快被委員會認識到。
Project managers emphasized the impracticableness of meeting the deadline. 項目經理們強調了在截止日期前完成的不可行性。
The project was impracticably designed with no attention to real-world constraints. 這個項目被設計得不切實際，沒有考慮現實世界的限制。
The timeline was impracticably optimistic, leading to inevitable delays. 時間表樂觀得不切實際，導致了不可避免的延遲。
Something described as impractical is not practical; impracticable. 被描述為不切實際的東西是不切實際的；不切實際的。
The word impractical is often used to describe a person, thing, or situation. 不切實際這個詞常用來形容人、事或情況。
The impracticality of wearing formal clothing to a construction site was obvious. 在建築工地穿正裝的不切實際性顯而易見。
The impracticality of the plan led the board to reject it. 計劃的不切實際性導致董事會否決了它。

meaning: 不切實際的性質（罕見詞）
sentences: The impracticalness of the idealized solution frustrated engineers. 理想化解決方案的不切實際性使工程師們感到沮喪。
The designer's impracticalness made her creations beautiful but unwearable. 設計師的不切實際性使她的作品漂亮卻無法穿戴。

meaning: 詛咒的；祈求禍患的（罕見詞）
sentences: The imprecant wizard was said to curse those who wronged him. 據說這位詛咒的巫師會詛咒那些傷害他的人。
His imprecant prayers against his enemies disturbed his community. 他對敵人的詛咒祈禱令他的社區感到不安。

meaning: 詛咒；祈求災難降臨（於某人）
sentences: He angrily imprecated his enemies with the most colorful curses. 他憤怒地以最生動的詛咒詛咒他的敵人。
In the story, the witch imprecated the village with seven years of drought. 在故事中，女巫詛咒村莊遭受七年乾旱。
He left the room in a fury, muttering imprecations under his breath. 他憤怒地離開了房間，低聲喃喃著詛咒。
The witch's imprecation was said to have caused the harvest to fail. 據說女巫的詛咒導致了收成失敗。
The imprecator was shunned by the village for his constant cursing. 這個詛咒者因不斷咒罵而被村民所迴避。
In the ancient world, the imprecator was sometimes hired to curse enemies. 在古代世界，詛咒者有時被雇來詛咒敵人。
He spoke imprecatorily about those who had betrayed him. 他以詛咒的方式談論那些背叛他的人。
The mob shouted imprecatorily at the gates of the palace. 暴徒在宮殿大門口以詛咒的方式高呼。
The imprecatory psalms in the Bible invoke divine judgment against enemies. 聖經中的詛咒詩篇呼求神對敵人的神聖審判。
His imprecatory language shocked even those who knew him well. 他的詛咒性語言甚至讓熟悉他的人感到震驚。
The imprecise measurements led to significant errors in the final product. 不精確的測量導致了最終產品中的重大錯誤。
His instructions were so imprecise that no one knew what to do next. 他的指示如此模糊，以至於沒有人知道下一步該怎麼做。
The concept was imprecisely defined in the first edition of the textbook. 這個概念在教科書的第一版中被不精確地定義了。
She imprecisely described the location, causing confusion among the rescue team. 她模糊地描述了位置，在救援隊伍中造成了混亂。
The imprecision of the data made it unreliable for policy decisions. 數據的不精確性使其對政策決定不可靠。
Imprecision in language can lead to serious misunderstandings. 語言中的模糊性可能導致嚴重的誤解。

meaning: 不可述說性；不可被謂述的特質（邏輯學）
sentences: The concept of impredicability arises in discussions of self-referential propositions. 不可述說性的概念出現在自我指涉命題的討論中。
Philosophers explore the impredicability of certain abstract entities. 哲學家探索某些抽象實體的不可述說性。

meaning: 不可被謂述的；不可述說的（邏輯學）
sentences: In formal logic, an impredicable set cannot define its own membership. 在形式邏輯中，不可被謂述的集合無法定義自己的成員資格。
The philosopher argued that the divine is impredicable by any human category. 哲學家論證神聖是無法用任何人類範疇來謂述的。

meaning: 使懷孕；使充滿（impregnate 的口語縮寫）
sentences: The term 'impreg' is sometimes used informally for impregnate in technical contexts. 「impreg」有時在技術語境中非正式地用於 impregnate（使充滿）。
The material was impreged with resin to increase its durability. 材料被用樹脂充滿以增加耐久性。
The wood was impregnated or impregned with preservative to resist decay. 木材被浸漬防腐劑以抵抗腐爛。
In medieval poetry, to impregn meant to cause something to be filled with a quality. 在中世紀詩歌中，impregn 意指使某物充滿某種品質。

meaning: 無法攻破性；堅不可摧性
sentences: The impregnability of the mountain fortress made it a safe refuge. 山地要塞的堅不可摧性使其成為安全的避難所。
The impregnability of his arguments was apparent to all the debaters. 他論點的無懈可擊性對所有辯論者都是顯而易見的。

meaning: 堅不可摧的；無法攻破的
sentences: The castle was considered impregnable due to its thick walls and strategic position. 由於其厚實的城牆和戰略位置，這座城堡被認為是堅不可摧的。
His logic was impregnable — no one could find a flaw in his argument. 他的邏輯是無懈可擊的——沒有人能在他的論點中找到缺陷。

meaning: 堅不可摧性（impregnability 的變體）
sentences: The impregnableness of the vault gave the bank confidence in its security. 保險庫的堅不可摧性使銀行對其安全性充滿信心。
Her impregnableness in debate earned her a reputation as an expert. 她在辯論中的無懈可擊性為她贏得了專家的聲譽。

meaning: 堅不可摧地；無懈可擊地
sentences: The fort stood impregnably against repeated assaults for three centuries. 這座要塞三個世紀以來堅不可摧地抵禦了一次又一次的攻擊。
His case was impregnably supported by documented evidence. 他的案件由有文件記錄的證據無懈可擊地支持著。
The impregnant was applied to the fabric to make it waterproof. 浸透劑被塗在面料上使其防水。
Researchers developed a new impregnant to protect against fire. 研究人員開發了一種新的浸透劑用於防火保護。
The wood was treated to impregnate it with a fire-resistant chemical. 木材被處理以使其浸透防火化學物質。
The soil was impregnated with nutrients to prepare it for planting. 土壤被充滿營養物質以為種植做準備。
The impregnation of the material with resin gave it extra strength. 用樹脂浸透材料賦予了它額外的強度。
In vitro impregnation is a procedure used in assisted reproduction. 體外受精是輔助生殖中使用的一種程序。
The impregnative solution penetrated deep into the wood fibers. 有滲透力的溶液深入滲透到木材纖維中。
The impregnative quality of the paint made it ideal for weatherproofing. 油漆的滲透性使其成為防風雨的理想選擇。
The industrial impregnator was used to coat wooden beams with preservative. 工業浸透裝置被用於在木梁上塗覆防腐劑。
The scientist served as the primary impregnator of the specimens in the research. 科學家在研究中擔任標本的主要浸透者。

meaning: 有浸透作用的；使充滿的（罕見詞）
sentences: The impregnatory liquid was designed to penetrate stone surfaces. 這種有浸透作用的液體被設計為能滲透石頭表面。
The impregnatory effect of the chemical improved the material's resistance. 化學物質的浸透效果改善了材料的抗性。

meaning: 無偏見；消除偏見（古體詞）
sentences: The judge was praised for his imprejudice in hearing all sides of the case. 法官因在審案中無偏見地聆聽所有方面而受到讚揚。
Scholars must cultivate imprejudice when examining evidence objectively. 學者在客觀審查證據時必須培養無偏見的態度。

meaning: 未經預謀的；無意識的（古體詞）
sentences: The impremeditate remark caused unintended offense. 未經預謀的言論造成了無意的冒犯。
His impremeditate decision to quit left everyone shocked. 他未經預謀地決定辭職讓所有人都感到震驚。

meaning: 未準備的狀態；欠缺準備（古體詞）
sentences: The army's impreparation for winter warfare led to catastrophic losses. 軍隊對冬季作戰的欠缺準備導致了災難性的損失。
Impreparation for emergencies can turn a minor crisis into a disaster. 對緊急情況欠缺準備可能會將小危機變成災難。

meaning: 格言圖案（由圖像和座右銘組成的個人紋章）
sentences: A Renaissance nobleman's impresa combined a symbolic image with a motto. 文藝復興貴族的格言圖案結合了象徵性圖像和座右銘。
The impresa was a popular form of personal symbolism in 16th-century Italy. 格言圖案在16世紀義大利是一種流行的個人象徵形式。

meaning: 演出經紀人；劇院製作人；主辦人
sentences: The famous impresario organized world tours for the greatest opera singers. 著名的演出經紀人為最偉大的歌劇歌手組織了世界巡迴演出。
As an impresario, she had an eye for discovering new talent. 作為演出經紀人，她有發現新才華的眼光。

meaning: 缺乏預知能力；無法預見（罕見詞）
sentences: The imprescience of the meteorologists led to an unprepared population during the storm. 氣象學家的缺乏預知能力導致了暴風雨期間民眾毫無準備。
No organization can avoid all failures given the inevitable imprescience of forecasts. 考慮到預測不可避免的缺乏預知能力，任何組織都無法避免所有失敗。

meaning: 不可被時效廢除的；不適用時效的（法律）
sentences: Human rights are considered imprescribable and cannot be extinguished by time. 人權被認為是不可被時效廢除的，不會因時間而消失。
Certain crimes are imprescribable and can be prosecuted at any time. 某些罪行不適用時效，可以在任何時候被起訴。

meaning: 不受時效限制的性質；永久性
sentences: The imprescriptibility of war crimes ensures perpetrators can always be prosecuted. 戰爭罪行不受時效限制的性質確保了犯罪者始終可以被起訴。
The imprescriptibility of certain rights is a cornerstone of international law. 某些權利不受時效限制的性質是國際法的基石。

meaning: 不受時效限制的；無法時效消滅的（法律）
sentences: The right to life is imprescriptible under international human rights law. 根據國際人權法，生命權是不受時效限制的。
War crimes are imprescriptible, meaning they can be tried at any future date. 戰爭罪行不受時效限制，意味著它們可以在未來任何日期被審判。

meaning: 以不受時效限制的方式
sentences: The crime was declared imprescriptibly serious by international tribunals. 國際法庭宣布這一罪行不受時效限制地嚴重。
Justice pursues crimes imprescriptibly when they violate universal human values. 當罪行違反普世人類價值觀時，正義以不受時效限制的方式追究。

meaning: 格言圖案（impresa 的複數或英語形式）
sentences: Imprese were common in Renaissance courts as symbols of a lord's character. 格言圖案在文藝復興宮廷中很常見，是領主品格的象徵。
The imprese used by the duke featured a falcon with a Latin motto. 公爵使用的格言圖案有一隻帶有拉丁座右銘的獵鷹。
He worked hard to impress his boss with his dedication and skills. 他努力工作，以他的奉獻精神與技能讓老闆留下深刻印象。
She managed to impress everyone with her brilliant presentation. 她成功以精彩的簡報給每個人留下了深刻印象。

meaning: 容易受印象影響的；可留下印記的
sentences: Young minds are highly impressable and easily influenced by their environment. 年輕的思想很容易受印象影響，容易受環境影響。
The impressable clay was shaped into a model before being fired. 容易留下印記的黏土在燒制前被塑成了模型。

meaning: 受到印象的；感動地（罕見詞）
sentences: He impressedly noted each new detail of the magnificent architecture. 他受到深刻印象地記錄了這座宏偉建築的每一個新細節。
She listened impressedly as the storyteller wove his tale. 當說書人編織他的故事時，她受到深刻印象地傾聽。

meaning: 施加印象的人；強制征兵者
sentences: The impresser worked for the navy, recruiting sailors by force if needed. 強制征兵者為海軍工作，必要時強制招募水手。
As an impresser of ideas, the teacher left a lasting mark on every student. 作為思想的施加者，老師在每個學生身上留下了持久的印記。

meaning: 易受影響性；容易留下印記的特質
sentences: The impressibility of young children makes early education so crucial. 年幼兒童容易受影響的特質使早期教育至關重要。
The impressibility of soft materials makes them suitable for molding. 柔軟材料容易留下印記的特質使其適合塑形。

meaning: 容易留下印記的；易受影響的
sentences: The impressible wax took the shape of the seal perfectly. 容易留下印記的蠟完美地呈現了印章的形狀。
Young people are impressible and benefit greatly from positive role models. 年輕人容易受影響，從積極的榜樣中獲益良多。

meaning: 易受影響性（impressibility 的變體）
sentences: The impressibleness of children is why storytelling has such a powerful impact. 兒童的易受影響性是為什麼說故事有如此強大影響力的原因。
The impressibleness of the medium allowed intricate patterns to be stamped into it. 這種材料的容易留下印記的特質允許複雜的圖案被壓印其中。

meaning: 容易受印象地；易受影響地（罕見詞）
sentences: She was impressibly moved by the speaker's personal story. 她被演講者的個人故事深深打動。
The audience listened impressibly as the orchestra performed the symphony. 當管弦樂團演奏交響樂時，觀眾深受感動地傾聽。
It's important to make a good first impression at an interview. 在工作面試中留下良好的第一印象是很重要的。
She has a very favorable impression of her new job and manager. 她對新工作與經理有著非常好的印象。

meaning: 容易受影響的特質；易感性
sentences: The impressionability of adolescents makes them vulnerable to peer pressure. 青少年的易感性使他們容易受到同伴壓力的影響。
Teachers recognize the impressionability of early childhood and use it for education. 教師認識到早期童年的易感性並將其用於教育。

meaning: 容易受影響的；易感動的
sentences: Young children are particularly impressionable and absorb everything around them. 幼兒特別容易受影響，吸收周圍的一切。
As an impressionable teenager, she was easily inspired by her teachers. 作為一個容易受影響的青少年，她很容易被老師們激勵。

meaning: 容易受影響性（impressionability 的變體）
sentences: The impressionableness of youth is both a strength and a vulnerability. 年輕人的容易受影響性既是優勢也是弱點。
The artist took advantage of the impressionableness of her material. 藝術家利用了她的材料容易留下印記的特質。

meaning: 容易受影響地；易被感動地（罕見詞）
sentences: He responded impressionably to any form of art or music he encountered. 他對遇到的任何藝術或音樂形式都容易受到影響。
The young student impressionably absorbed the lessons of the master. 這位年輕學生容易受影響地吸收了大師的課程。
The impressional quality of the painting evoked strong emotional responses. 這幅畫的印象性質引發了強烈的情感反應。
Impressional learning focuses on how external experiences shape inner life. 印象性學習關注外部經驗如何塑造內心生活。

meaning: 印象派藝術家；印象主義者
sentences: As an impressionalist, Monet was known for his use of light and color. 作為一位印象派藝術家，莫內以其對光線和色彩的使用而聞名。
The impressionalist painter sought to capture fleeting moments rather than precise detail. 印象主義畫家試圖捕捉轉瞬即逝的時刻而非精確的細節。

meaning: 印象性；給人留下印象的特質（罕見詞）
sentences: The impressionality of the performance lingered in the audience's memory for weeks. 表演的印象性在觀眾的記憶中留存了幾週。
Impressionality in art is valued for its emotional resonance rather than accuracy. 藝術中的印象性因其情感共鳴而非準確性被珍視。

meaning: 在印象上；以產生印象的方式（罕見詞）
sentences: The speech was impressionally powerful, leaving all who heard it changed. 這次演講在印象上非常有力量，讓所有聽到的人都發生了改變。
The music works impressionally, conveying emotion rather than narrative. 這首音樂以產生印象的方式運作，傳達情感而非敘事。

meaning: 印象性的；留下印象的（罕見詞）
sentences: The impressionary style of the poem resists literal interpretation. 這首詩的印象性風格抗拒字面解讀。
Impressionary art invites viewers to feel rather than analyze. 印象性藝術邀請觀者感受而非分析。

meaning: 印象主義（藝術運動）；印象派風格
sentences: Impressionism emerged in France in the 1870s as a revolutionary art movement. 印象主義在1870年代作為革命性藝術運動在法國興起。
Impressionism in music seeks to evoke atmospheric moods rather than precise emotions. 音樂中的印象主義試圖喚起氛圍性的情緒而非精確的感情。

meaning: 印象派藝術家；印象主義者；印象派的
sentences: Renoir was a celebrated impressionist painter renowned for his vibrant scenes. 雷諾瓦是一位著名的印象派畫家，以其充滿活力的場景而聞名。
The comedian's impressionist skills allowed her to mimic dozens of celebrities. 這位喜劇演員的印象主義技巧使她能夠模仿幾十位名人。
His impressionistic account of events differed from the official record. 他對事件的印象派描述與官方記錄不同。
The novel is written in an impressionistic style that blurs time and space. 這部小說以模糊時間和空間的印象派風格寫成。

meaning: 以印象派方式；依印象地
sentences: The film was impressionistically edited to reflect the protagonist's mental state. 這部電影以印象派方式剪輯，以反映主角的精神狀態。
She wrote impressionistically, capturing the mood rather than the facts. 她以印象派方式寫作，捕捉情緒而非事實。

meaning: 沒有留下印象的；平淡無奇的
sentences: The speech was so poorly delivered it was entirely impressionless. 這次演講表達得如此糟糕，完全沒有留下任何印象。
An impressionless performance is quickly forgotten by audiences. 平淡無奇的表演很快就會被觀眾遺忘。
She gave a very impressive presentation at the conference. 她在會議上做了一場令人印象深刻的簡報。
The new library building is a truly impressive structure. 這座新的圖書館大樓簡直是一座令人讚嘆的建築。

meaning: 令人印象深刻地；壯觀地
sentences: She impressively solved the complex math problem in under a minute. 她令人印象深刻地在一分鐘內解決了複雜的數學問題。
The building stood impressively tall against the skyline. 這棟建築壯觀地高聳在天際線旁。

meaning: 令人印象深刻的特質；壯觀
sentences: The impressiveness of the Grand Canyon never fails to awe visitors. 大峽谷令人印象深刻的特質總是讓遊客驚嘆。
The impressiveness of the performance earned the cast a standing ovation. 表演的令人印象深刻的特質為演員們贏得了起立鼓掌。

meaning: 強制征兵；強制服役（尤指海軍）
sentences: The impressment of sailors by the British Royal Navy was a cause of the War of 1812. 英國皇家海軍對水手的強制征兵是1812年戰爭的起因之一。
Impressment into military service was deeply resented by the common people. 被強制服役深受普通百姓的憎恨。

meaning: 施加印記的人；印刷機操作員（古體詞）
sentences: The impressor applied a wax seal to the official documents. 印記施加者在官方文件上蓋上了蠟封印。
As the impressor of the royal mint, he oversaw the production of coins. 作為皇家鑄幣廠的印刷機操作員，他監督硬幣的生產。
The impressure of the seal was still visible on the ancient wax. 印章的印記在古老的蠟上仍然清晰可見。
The impressure of childhood experiences can last a lifetime. 童年經歷的印象可以持續一生。

meaning: 預支款項；備用金（財務術語）
sentences: The imprest system allows employees to advance funds for small expenses. 備用金制度允許員工預支資金用於小額支出。
The petty cash account operates as an imprest fund in the company. 零用現金帳戶作為公司的備用金基金運作。

meaning: 可預支的；可預付的（罕見詞）
sentences: The amount was deemed imprestable under the company's financial policy. 根據公司的財務政策，這筆金額被認為是可預支的。
Travel expenses were imprestable before the business trip. 出差前，差旅費用可以預支。

meaning: 無法預防性；不可避免性（罕見詞）
sentences: The impreventability of the accident highlighted flaws in the safety protocol. 事故的無法預防性突顯了安全規程中的缺陷。
Philosophers discuss the impreventability of moral failure in a flawed world. 哲學家討論在有缺陷的世界中道德失敗的不可避免性。

meaning: 無法預防的；不可避免的（罕見詞）
sentences: Some accidents are impreventable despite the best safety measures. 儘管有最好的安全措施，有些事故仍然無法預防。
The flood was deemed impreventable given the extreme rainfall that year. 考慮到當年的極端降雨量，洪水被認為是無法預防的。

meaning: 不可預見性；難以預測的特質（罕見詞）
sentences: The imprevisibility of the market made long-term investment risky. 市場的不可預見性使長期投資風險很高。
The imprevisibility of weather patterns challenges agricultural planning. 天氣模式的難以預測的特質挑戰了農業規劃。

meaning: 不可預見的；難以預測的（罕見詞）
sentences: The earthquake was imprevisible and caught the city unprepared. 地震是不可預見的，讓城市措手不及。
Human behavior can be imprevisible, defying even the most sophisticated models. 人類行為可能是難以預測的，挑戰了即使最複雜的模型。

meaning: 缺乏預見；不謹慎（古體詞）
sentences: The general's imprevision of enemy tactics led to a costly defeat. 將軍對敵方戰術缺乏預見導致了代價高昂的失敗。
Imprevision in financial planning can lead to insolvency. 財務規劃中缺乏預見可能導致破產。

meaning: 出版許可；認可；官方批准
sentences: The book received the church's imprimatur before it could be published. 這本書在出版之前獲得了教會的出版許可。
The project received the imprimatur of the board of directors. 該項目獲得了董事會的認可。
Gutenberg's press allowed books to be imprimeed quickly and in great numbers. 古騰堡的印刷機使書籍能夠快速大量地被印刷。
The king ordered his seal to be imprimeed on every official document. 國王命令將他的印章施加在每一份官方文件上。

meaning: 非原始的；不具基本性的（數學）
sentences: An imprimitive group in mathematics has non-trivial block systems. 數學中的非原始群具有非平凡的塊系統。
The algebraic structure was classified as imprimitive after detailed analysis. 代數結構在詳細分析後被分類為非原始的。

meaning: 非原始性（數學群論術語）
sentences: Imprimitivity in group theory refers to the existence of non-trivial invariant partitions. 群論中的非原始性是指非平凡不變劃分的存在。
The concept of imprimitivity is central to the study of permutation groups. 非原始性的概念是置換群研究的核心。

meaning: 一個印象；印某物留下的痕跡。
sentences: The day left an imprint in my mind. 那一天在我的腦海裡留下了深刻的印象。
The term imprint refers to an impression; the mark left behind by printing something. 印記一詞是指印記；印某物時留下的痕跡。

meaning: 打印機；壓印器；留下印記的人或物
sentences: The credit card imprinter was an older device used before digital systems. 信用卡壓印器是數字系統出現之前使用的較舊設備。
The imprinter stamped each document with the official seal. 壓印器在每份文件上蓋上了官方印章。
The dissident was imprisoned for speaking out against the government. 異見人士因對政府發聲而被監禁。
He felt imprisoned by his own fears and could not move forward. 他感到被自己的恐懼所囚禁，無法前進。
The suspect was imprisoned following his conviction for the robbery. 嫌疑人在搶劫罪名成立後被監禁。
The court sentenced the criminal to ten years of imprisonment. 法院判處該罪犯十年監禁。

meaning: 可被監禁的；應受監禁懲罰的
sentences: The offense was imprisonable with a maximum sentence of five years. 這一罪行可被監禁，最高刑期為五年。
Not all crimes are imprisonable — some carry only fines. 並非所有罪行都可監禁——有些只需繳納罰款。
The unjust imprisoner was later put on trial for abuse of power. 不公正的監禁者後來因濫用職權而受到審判。
Throughout history, political imprisoners have often been celebrated as heroes. 縱觀歷史，政治監禁者往往被稱頌為英雄。

meaning: 監禁在某個地方，尤指監獄或看守所，尤指作為對犯罪的懲罰。
sentences: The term imprisonment refers to a confinement in a place, especially a prison or a jail, especially as punishment for a crime. 監禁一詞指的是在一個地方，特別是監獄或看守所的監禁，特別是作為對犯罪的懲罰。
The lesson introduced imprisonment as a useful term to understand. 本課介紹了監禁這個易於理解的有用術語。

meaning: 不可能性；難以置信的事
sentences: The improbability of finding life in outer space continues to fascinate scientists. 在外太空發現生命的不可能性繼續讓科學家著迷。
The improbability of his survival against the odds inspired many. 他違反所有odds的生存的難以置信性激勵了許多人。

meaning: 使不可能；降低可能性（罕見詞）
sentences: The new data served to improbabilize the original hypothesis. 新數據降低了原始假設的可能性。
Bad weather can improbabilize the success of an outdoor event. 惡劣天氣可以降低戶外活動成功的可能性。
It's highly improbable that aliens abducted you. 外星人綁架你的可能性極低。
Something described as improbable is not likely to be true. 被描述為不可能的事情不太可能是真的。
The improbableness of the scenario made it hard for scientists to study. 這個場景的不可能性使科學家難以研究。
Despite its improbableness, the plan succeeded against all odds. 儘管其不可能性，這個計劃違反所有概率成功了。
The tiny startup improbably grew into a global corporation within ten years. 這家小型初創公司難以置信地在十年內成長為一家全球公司。
The underdog team improbably won the championship against all expectations. 弱隊違反所有預期地難以置信地贏得了冠軍。

meaning: 否認；不批准；拒絕承認（古體詞）
sentences: The improbation of the false document was upheld by the court. 法院支持了對假文件的拒絕承認。
His improbation of the accusation was steadfast throughout the investigation. 在整個調查過程中，他對指控的否認始終堅定。

meaning: 否認的；不批准的（罕見詞）
sentences: His improbative testimony contradicted the prosecution's claims. 他否認性的證詞與檢察官的主張相矛盾。
An improbative stance toward new evidence can hinder justice. 對新證據採取否認的立場可能阻礙正義。

meaning: 否認性的；表示不批准的（罕見詞）
sentences: The improbatory remarks of the critic dismissed the artist's entire body of work. 批評家否認性的言論否定了藝術家的全部作品。
The improbatory tone of the response suggested the proposal would be rejected. 回應的否認性語氣表明該提案將被拒絕。
The politician was charged with improbity in office for accepting bribes. 這位政治家因在職期間接受賄賂而被指控不誠實。
Improbity in public administration erodes citizen trust in government. 公共行政中的不誠實侵蝕了公民對政府的信任。

meaning: 不能生育的；無繁殖力的（古體詞）
sentences: The improcreant creature in the myth could only be destroyed, not reproduced. 神話中無法繁殖的生物只能被消滅，不能再生。
The ancient text described the stone as improcreant — capable of no growth. 古代文本描述這塊石頭為無繁殖力的——無法生長。

meaning: 無法取得性；難以獲得的特質（罕見詞）
sentences: The improcurability of the rare ingredient drove up the cost of production. 這種稀有成分的無法取得性推高了生產成本。
The improcurability of justice in corrupt systems is a recurring theme. 在腐敗體制中無法取得正義是一個反覆出現的主題。

meaning: 無法取得的；難以獲得的（罕見詞）
sentences: The legendary herb was considered improcurable by most apothecaries. 這種傳奇草藥被大多數藥劑師認為是無法取得的。
In wartime, many everyday goods became improcurable due to shortages. 在戰時，由於短缺，許多日常物品變得難以獲得。

meaning: 無法生產的；無法展示的（罕見詞）
sentences: The evidence was declared improducible as it had been destroyed. 證據因已被銷毀而被宣布無法展示。
The technology required to build it was still improducible at the time. 建造它所需的技術在當時仍然是無法生產的。

meaning: 能力不足；技藝不精（罕見詞）
sentences: The improficience of new recruits required months of additional training. 新兵的能力不足需要幾個月的額外培訓。
His improficience in the foreign language made negotiations difficult. 他在外語方面技藝不精使談判變得困難。

meaning: 不熟練；技術不足（improficience 的變體）
sentences: The improficiency of the staff led to a string of costly errors. 員工的不熟練導致了一系列代價高昂的錯誤。
Addressing improficiency through training can transform an organization. 通過培訓解決不熟練問題可以改造一個組織。

meaning: 非進步的；停滯不前的（罕見詞）
sentences: The improgressive attitude of the management frustrated innovative employees. 管理層停滯不前的態度使具有創新精神的員工感到沮喪。
Improgressive policies hold back economic development. 非進步的政策阻礙了經濟發展。

meaning: 以非進步的方式；停滯地（罕見詞）
sentences: The organization operated improgressively for decades, resisting all change. 該組織幾十年來以停滯不前的方式運作，抵制一切變革。
She improgressively managed the team, always relying on outdated methods. 她以非進步的方式管理團隊，總是依賴過時的方法。

meaning: 停滯不前；非進步性（罕見詞）
sentences: The improgressiveness of the system was blamed for the country's slow development. 體制的停滯不前被歸咎為國家發展緩慢的原因。
Critics wrote about the improgressiveness of cultural attitudes in the region. 批評家撰文談論該地區文化態度的非進步性。

meaning: 不多產的；缺乏創造力的（極罕見詞）
sentences: The improlificical writer produced only a handful of works during his lifetime. 這位不多產的作家一生只創作了少量作品。
The improlificical season for the crops was due to the persistent drought. 農作物不多產的季節是由持續的乾旱造成的。

meaning: 即興性；即席的特質（罕見詞）
sentences: The impromptitude of his speech surprised everyone in the audience. 他演講的即興性令在場所有人感到驚訝。
Her impromptitude in music allowed her to perform without preparation. 她在音樂上的即興性使她能夠在未準備的情況下表演。

meaning: 即興創作；未經事先準備、規劃或排練。
sentences: The party began with an impromptu rendition of 'Happy Birthday'. 晚會在即興表演的《生日快樂》中開始。
an impromptu speech 即興演講

meaning: 即興手冊；隨機應答的集子（罕見詞）
sentences: The comedian kept an impromptuary of jokes for unexpected performances. 那位喜劇演員保存著一本即興笑話集，以備臨時表演。
His impromptuary of witty remarks impressed dinner party guests. 他那本機智言論集讓晚宴賓客留下深刻印象。

meaning: 即興表演者；擅長即興的人
sentences: As an impromptuist, she could compose poems on any topic instantly. 作為即興表演者，她能即時就任何主題創作詩歌。
The impromptuist entertained the crowd with spontaneous speeches. 即興表演者以即席演講娛樂人群。
The improof of the system was achieved through years of dedicated research. 這個系統的改進是通過多年的專注研究實現的。
He dedicated his career to the improof of agricultural methods. 他將職業生涯獻給了農業方法的改善。

meaning: 不符合傳統習俗或良好舉止；不雅或不謙虛的。
sentences: improper conduct 不當行為
Something described as improper is not in keeping with conventional mores or good manners; indecent or immodest. 被描述為不當的行為不符合傳統習俗或良好舉止；不雅或不謙虛的。
The teacher's improperation shamed the students into better behavior. 老師的責備使學生感到羞恥，從而表現得更好。
His improperation of the lazy workers made little difference. 他對懶惰工人的斥責收效甚微。

meaning: 以不正當的方式；不正確。
sentences: If someone acts improperly, they do so in a way that is in an improper manner; not properly. 如果某人的行為不正當，那麼他們的行為方式就是不正當的；不正確。
The speaker responded improperly during the discussion. 發言者在討論過程中回應不當。
The improperness of his comments during the ceremony was widely criticized. 他在典禮上評論的不適當性受到廣泛批評。
She was unaware of the improperness of her behavior in that cultural context. 她沒有意識到在那種文化背景下她行為的不合宜性。

meaning: 侵占（教會財產）；不適當地佔用
sentences: In medieval England, lords would sometimes impropriate church revenues for personal use. 在中世紀英格蘭，領主有時會侵占教會收入供個人使用。
The estate was impropriated by the crown following the dissolution of the monastery. 修道院解散後，這份地產被王室佔用了。

meaning: 教會財產的世俗化；侵占教會收益
sentences: The impropriation of church lands was common during the English Reformation. 英國宗教改革期間，教會土地的世俗化很普遍。
Historians debate the economic effects of impropriation on rural communities. 歷史學家討論教會財產世俗化對農村社區的經濟影響。

meaning: 侵占教會財產的世俗人士
sentences: The impropriator who seized the abbey lands was later condemned by the church. 侵占修道院土地的世俗人士後來受到教會的譴責。
As an impropriator, he collected tithes that once belonged to the parish priest. 作為侵占者，他收取曾屬於教區神父的什一稅。

meaning: 女性侵占教會財產者（impropriator 的陰性形式）
sentences: The wealthy impropriatrix controlled several church livings in the region. 富有的女性侵占者控制著該地區幾個教會的聖職俸祿。
Historical records mention an impropriatrix who managed abbey lands after the Reformation. 歷史記錄提到了一位在宗教改革後管理修道院土地的女性侵占者。

meaning: 不適當；不得體；不合宜的行為
sentences: His impropriety at the formal dinner embarrassed his colleagues. 他在正式晚宴上的不得體行為讓同事感到難堪。
The judge dismissed the case due to procedural impropriety. 法官因程序上的不適當而駁回了此案。
The improvability of the process was demonstrated through successive experiments. 通過連續的實驗展示了這個過程的可改進性。
She believed in the improvability of human character through education. 她相信通過教育可以提升人的品格。
The coach believed that every athlete's performance was improvable with practice. 教練相信每位運動員的表現都是可以通過練習來提升的。
Scientists argued that the technology was improvable given more funding. 科學家們認為，只要有更多資金，這項技術是可以改進的。

meaning: 可改進的性質；可提升性
sentences: The improvableness of the design encouraged engineers to keep refining it. 設計的可改進性鼓勵工程師繼續完善它。
Teachers cherished the improvableness they saw in their students. 老師們珍視他們在學生身上看到的可提升性。

meaning: 可改進地；以可提升的方式
sentences: The system was improvably designed, with updates released regularly. 該系統被設計為可持續改進的，定期發布更新。
She performed improvably in each test, showing steady progress. 她在每次測試中都有可見的進步，顯示出穩定的成長。
I hope to improve my English skills this semester. 我希望這學期能提高我的英語技能。
The company is working hard to improve its products. 公司正努力改善其產品。
I'm taking an online course to improve my speaking skills. 我正修一門線上課程以提升我的口說能力。
There has been a significant improvement in his health lately. 他的健康最近有了顯著的改善。
There has been a significant improvement in her health. 她的健康狀況有了顯著改善。
The new model is a major improvement over the old one. 新款比舊款有很大的進步。

meaning: 改進者；學徒期結束後仍繼續學習的人
sentences: As a keen improver, she spent extra hours studying advanced techniques. 作為一個熱心的進修者，她花了額外的時間學習高級技巧。
The improver was hired to enhance the factory's production methods. 這位改進者被聘用來提升工廠的生產方法。

meaning: 改進者的身份或職位（罕見詞）
sentences: His improvership within the guild gave him access to advanced training. 他在行會中的改進者身份使他能夠接受進階訓練。
The improvership lasted two years before he became a full journeyman. 他在成為正式工匠前，擔任了兩年的改進者。

meaning: 缺乏遠見；揮霍無度；不節儉
sentences: His improvidence with money left him in debt by middle age. 他在金錢上的揮霍無度使他在中年時陷入債務。
The improvidence of the government in planning for emergencies was criticized. 政府在應急規劃上缺乏遠見受到了批評。

meaning: 缺乏遠見的；不節儉的；揮霍的
sentences: The improvident spender never saved anything for retirement. 這個揮霍無度的花費者從未為退休儲蓄任何東西。
Being improvident with natural resources can lead to long-term environmental damage. 對自然資源缺乏節制可能導致長期的環境破壞。

meaning: 缺乏遠見地；不節儉地（古體詞）
sentences: He improvidentially spent his inheritance within a year of receiving it. 他在收到遺產後一年內便揮霍一空。
The resources were improvidentially managed, leading to unnecessary waste. 這些資源被缺乏遠見地管理，導致了不必要的浪費。
She improvidently quit her job without having another one lined up. 她在沒有其他工作安排的情況下草率地辭職了。
The funds were improvidently allocated, causing a budget crisis later. 資金被缺乏遠見地分配，後來引發了預算危機。
The patient showed improving symptoms after starting the new treatment. 患者在開始新治療後顯示出改善的症狀。
She looked for improving literature to help develop her mind. 她尋找有益的文學作品來幫助發展自己的思想。

meaning: 以改善的方式；有益地（罕見詞）
sentences: The teacher spoke improvingly, always aiming to better her students. 老師以有益的方式說話，總是致力於提升她的學生。
He improvingly redesigned the workflow to reduce inefficiencies. 他以改善的方式重新設計了工作流程以減少低效率。

meaning: 即興創作；即興表演（improvise 的同義詞）
sentences: The musician could improvisate a melody on any instrument. 那位音樂家能在任何樂器上即興創作旋律。
She had to improvisate a solution when the equipment failed. 當設備故障時，她不得不即興想出解決方案。

meaning: 即興創作和創作音樂、詩歌等的行為或藝術
sentences: He played a quick improvisation on the keyboard. 他在鍵盤上快速 即興演奏 。 他在鍵盤上快速即興演奏。 他在鍵盤上即時興播放。

The term improvisation refers to The act or art of composing and making music, poetry, and the like, extemporaneously. 即興創作一詞是指即興創作和創作音樂、詩歌等的行為或藝術， 即興創作 。 即興創作一詞是指即興創作和創作音樂、詩歌等的行為或藝術。 即興創作是指即興創作和創作音樂、詩歌等行為或藝術，即興創作。
Improvisational jazz relies on musicians responding to each other in real time. 即興爵士樂依賴音樂家實時相互回應。
The improvisational comedy show had the audience in stitches all night. 即興喜劇表演讓觀眾整晚捧腹大笑。

meaning: 即興詩人；即興表演者（尤指義大利傳統）
sentences: The improvisator amazed the court with his ability to compose verses on demand. 即興詩人憑藉按需創作詩句的能力令宮廷驚嘆。
The tradition of the improvisator was especially popular in 18th-century Italy. 即興詩人的傳統在18世紀的義大利特別流行。

meaning: 即興的；即興詩人風格的
sentences: His improvisatorial talent was admired throughout the literary salons. 他的即興才能在整個文學沙龍中受到欽佩。
The improvisatorial style of the lecture kept students engaged. 演講的即興風格使學生保持投入。

meaning: 以即興詩人的方式；即興地
sentences: He spoke improvisatorially, weaving facts and anecdotes together seamlessly. 他以即興的方式演講，將事實和軼事無縫地交織在一起。
The performer composed improvisatorially, creating music without preparation. 表演者以即興的方式創作，無需準備就創作音樂。

meaning: 以即興詩人的方式表演；進行即興創作（罕見詞）
sentences: He would improvisatorize at parties, entertaining guests with witty rhymes. 他在派對上會即興表演，用機智的押韻娛樂賓客。
The poet was invited to improvisatorize before the gathered nobility. 詩人被邀請在聚集的貴族面前即興創作。
The pianist's improvisatory style made each performance unique. 鋼琴家的即興風格使每次表演都獨一無二。
Her improvisatory remarks during the speech were surprisingly insightful. 她在演講中的即興言論出人意料地富有洞察力。

meaning: 隨心所欲地編造或發明某物；僅以想像力、直覺和猜測為指導，而不是透過仔細的計劃來進行。
sentences: He had no speech prepared, so he improvised. 他沒有準備好演講，所以他即興演講。
They improvised a simple shelter with branches and the rope they were carrying. 他們用樹枝和攜帶的繩子臨時搭建了一個簡單的避難所。
The actor delivered his speech improvisedly after forgetting his lines. 演員在忘記台詞後即興地發表了他的演講。
She improvisedly solved the crisis without any prior planning. 她在沒有任何事先計劃的情況下即興解決了危機。

meaning: 即興表演者；即興創作者
sentences: As an expert improviser, he could adapt to any situation on stage. 作為一位專業的即興表演者，他能夠在舞台上適應任何情況。
The jazz improviser created a new melody with every performance. 爵士即興演奏者在每次表演中創作出新的旋律。

meaning: 即興創作；即興表演（improvisation 的變體）
sentences: His talent for improvision made him one of the most sought-after performers. 他在即興創作方面的才能使他成為最受歡迎的表演者之一。
Improvision in theater requires both skill and confidence. 劇場中的即興表演需要技巧和自信。

meaning: 即興作品；即興曲（音樂術語）
sentences: The composer played an improviso that left the audience breathless. 作曲家演奏了一首令觀眾屏息的即興曲。
An improviso is a musical piece created spontaneously without prior preparation. 即興曲是在沒有事先準備的情況下即興創作的音樂作品。

meaning: 即興表演者；即興詩人（improviser 的變體拼法）
sentences: The improvisor was known for his ability to compose verse on any given subject. 即興詩人以其能夠就任何給定主題創作詩句的能力而聞名。
As an improvisor, she could turn any word into a full poem in minutes. 作為即興創作者，她能在幾分鐘內將任何單詞變成一首完整的詩。
His imprudence in financial matters led to serious losses. 他在財務事務上的輕率導致了嚴重的損失。
The imprudence of the decision became apparent only after the damage was done. 這個決定的輕率在損害造成後才變得明顯。

meaning: 輕率；不謹慎（imprudence 的舊式用法）
sentences: Her imprudency in sharing confidential information cost the company dearly. 她在分享機密信息上的輕率使公司付出了高昂代價。
The imprudency of youth often leads to regrettable decisions. 年輕的輕率常常導致令人遺憾的決定。

meaning: 輕率的；不謹慎的；欠考慮的
sentences: It was imprudent to make such large investments without proper research. 在沒有適當研究的情況下進行如此大額的投資是輕率的。
His imprudent remarks at the meeting created unnecessary tension. 他在會議上輕率的言論造成了不必要的緊張氣氛。

meaning: 缺乏審慎的；不符合謹慎原則的（罕見詞）
sentences: The imprudential handling of the negotiations led to their collapse. 談判的不謹慎處理導致了談判的崩潰。
An imprudential approach to risk management can have severe consequences. 不謹慎的風險管理方式可能產生嚴重後果。
She imprudently shared her password with an untrusted colleague. 她輕率地與一位不可信賴的同事分享了她的密碼。
He acted imprudently by signing the contract without reading it. 他在未閱讀合約的情況下輕率地簽了字。

meaning: 輕率的性質；不謹慎性（罕見詞）
sentences: The imprudentness of his driving resulted in a serious accident. 他駕駛時的輕率導致了一場嚴重事故。
Her imprudentness in matters of trust alienated her closest friends. 她在信任問題上的輕率使她最親密的朋友疏遠了。

meaning: 小惡魔的品質；淘氣鬼的行為（罕見詞）
sentences: The child's impship delighted his grandparents even as it exasperated his parents. 孩子的淘氣鬼行為讓他的祖父母感到高興，儘管這讓他的父母惱怒。
There was an endearing impship about his pranks that won people over. 他惡作劇中有一種令人喜愛的頑皮特質，能夠贏得人心。

meaning: 未成年的；青春期前的（醫學/法律術語）
sentences: The impuberal child was too young for the legal proceedings. 這個未成年的孩子太小，無法參與法律程序。
Impuberal patients require specialized medical care different from adults. 青春期前的患者需要與成人不同的專科醫療照護。

meaning: 未到達青春期的；未成熟的（醫學術語）
sentences: Impuberate individuals are legally protected from certain adult responsibilities. 未達青春期的個人在法律上受到保護，免於承擔某些成人責任。
The doctor noted that the patient was still impuberate based on developmental markers. 醫生根據發育指標指出患者仍未到達青春期。

meaning: 青春期前的狀態；未成年（醫學術語）
sentences: Legal systems often make provisions for individuals in a state of impuberty. 法律制度通常對處於青春期前狀態的個人有特殊規定。
Impuberty is recognized in law as a condition requiring additional protections. 在法律上，青春期前的狀態被認為是需要額外保護的條件。

meaning: 未發育恥骨毛髮的；青春期前的（醫學術語）
sentences: The term impubic is used in medical contexts to describe pre-adolescent development. 「impubic」這個術語在醫學背景下用於描述青春期前的發育。
Growth records distinguish between impubic and pubescent stages of development. 成長記錄區分了青春期前和青春期的發育階段。
The student's impudence in talking back to the teacher shocked the class. 學生對老師頂嘴的無禮行為震驚了全班。
She had the impudence to demand a refund after deliberately damaging the item. 她厚顏無恥地在故意損壞商品後要求退款。

meaning: 無禮；厚顏無恥（impudence 的舊式變體）
sentences: His impudency in interrupting the speaker angered everyone present. 他打斷演講者的無禮行為激怒了所有在場的人。
The impudency of his request left the committee members speechless. 他請求的厚顏無恥讓委員會成員們瞠目結舌。

meaning: 無禮的；厚顏無恥的；冒失的
sentences: The impudent child made faces at his teacher behind her back. 那個無禮的孩子在老師背後對她做鬼臉。
It was impudent of him to contradict the expert in front of everyone. 他在眾人面前反駁這位專家是很冒失的。
She impudently demanded to see the manager without having an appointment. 她厚顏無恥地要求在沒有預約的情況下見經理。
He impudently cut in line, ignoring the protests of others. 他無禮地插隊，無視他人的抗議。

meaning: 無禮的特質；厚顏無恥性（罕見詞）
sentences: His impudentness was a constant source of friction in the office. 他的無禮特質是辦公室裡持續摩擦的來源。
The impudentness of her remarks offended even her closest friends. 她言論的無禮特質甚至冒犯了她最親密的朋友。

meaning: 無恥；淫蕩；不知羞恥（古體詞）
sentences: The impudicity of the performance shocked the conservative audience. 表演的無恥行為震驚了保守的觀眾。
In earlier centuries, impudicity was considered a serious moral failing. 在早期的幾個世紀，不知羞恥被視為嚴重的道德缺陷。

meaning: 質疑；挑戰；反駁（某人的誠信或動機）
sentences: The defense attorney sought to impugn the credibility of the witness. 辯護律師試圖質疑證人的可信度。
It is unfair to impugn someone's motives without solid evidence. 在沒有確鑿證據的情況下質疑某人的動機是不公平的。

meaning: 可被質疑的性質；可被反駁性
sentences: The impugnability of the contract clause led to a lengthy legal dispute. 合約條款的可被質疑性導致了漫長的法律糾紛。
Scholars noted the impugnability of certain assumptions in the theory. 學者們指出了理論中某些假設的可被質疑性。

meaning: 可被質疑的；可被反駁的
sentences: The impugnable testimony weakened the prosecution's case significantly. 可被質疑的證詞大大削弱了檢察官的案件。
His claims were impugnable due to the lack of supporting evidence. 由於缺乏支持證據，他的主張是可被質疑的。
The impugnation of the scientist's findings sparked widespread debate. 對科學家發現的質疑引發了廣泛的討論。
The lawyer's impugnation of the witness's character proved effective. 律師對證人品格的質疑被證明是有效的。
As an impugner of established theories, he was both respected and criticized. 作為既有理論的質疑者，他既受到尊重也受到批評。
The impugner of the testimony failed to provide convincing counter-evidence. 質疑證詞的人未能提供令人信服的反面證據。
The impugnment of the official's integrity led to an investigation. 對官員廉潔性的質疑導致了一次調查。
His impugnment of the research methods undermined the study's credibility. 他對研究方法的質疑損害了研究的可信度。

meaning: 無力；軟弱；無能為力（古體詞）
sentences: His impuissance in the face of the crisis left everyone desperate. 他在危機面前的無能為力使所有人感到絕望。
The impuissance of the small nation against its powerful neighbor was evident. 小國面對其強大鄰國的無力感是顯而易見的。

meaning: 無力的；軟弱的；無能為力的（古體詞）
sentences: The impuissant king could do nothing as his kingdom fell to invaders. 無力的國王在他的王國陷落於侵略者時束手無策。
She felt impuissant against the bureaucratic obstacles she faced. 她感到在面對官僚障礙時無能為力。
He had a sudden impulse to quit his job and travel around the world. 他突然有一種辭職去環遊世界的衝動。
I bought this dress on impulse, and now I'm not sure if I really like it. 我衝動之下買了這件洋裝，現在我不確定自己是否真的喜歡它。
He acted on impulsion, buying the car without thinking about the costs. 他衝動行事，在沒有考慮費用的情況下買了這輛車。
The impulsion to explore drove early navigators across unknown seas. 探索的衝動驅使早期航海家穿越未知的海洋。
She is an impulsive buyer who often spends more than she can afford. 她是個衝動的購物者，經常入不敷出。
His impulsive decision led to many problems. 他衝動的決定導致了許多問題。
She impulsively quit her job without having another lined up. 她衝動地辭職，沒有事先安排好另一份工作。
He impulsively agreed to help before understanding what was involved. 他在不了解所涉及內容的情況下衝動地同意了幫忙。
His impulsiveness often got him into trouble in social situations. 他的衝動性常常在社交場合讓他陷入麻煩。
The therapist worked with her to manage her impulsiveness. 治療師幫助她控制她的衝動性。

meaning: 衝動性；衝動傾向（心理學術語）
sentences: High impulsivity is often associated with attention deficit disorder. 高度衝動性通常與注意力缺陷症有關。
Researchers studied impulsivity as a factor in risky decision-making. 研究人員研究衝動性作為冒險決策的一個因素。

meaning: 具有推動力的；激勵性的（罕見詞）
sentences: The impulsory nature of the reward system motivated employees effectively. 獎勵制度的激勵性有效地激勵了員工。
An impulsory force drove the invention of the steam engine. 一種具有推動力的力量推動了蒸汽機的發明。

meaning: 無點的；無斑點的（生物學術語）
sentences: The impunctate surface of the shell made it unusual among its species. 貝殼無斑點的表面使其在同類中顯得不尋常。
Biologists classified the specimen as impunctate based on its smooth exterior. 生物學家根據其光滑的外表將標本分類為無斑點的。
Her impunctual habits made her colleagues frustrated and unreliable. 她不守時的習慣讓她的同事感到沮喪，使她變得不可靠。
The impunctual delivery of supplies disrupted the entire production schedule. 物資不準時的交付打亂了整個生產計劃。
His impunctuality was a constant source of frustration to his team. 他的不守時是他團隊持續沮喪的根源。
The impunctuality of the trains made commuters plan extra travel time. 火車的不準時使通勤者計劃了額外的出行時間。

meaning: 不受懲罰地；無後果地（罕見詞）
sentences: The corrupt official acted impunely for years before being caught. 這位腐敗的官員在被抓之前不受懲罰地行事多年。
He stole impunely because no one dared to report him. 他不受懲罰地偷竊，因為沒有人敢舉報他。

meaning: 不受懲罰的；不可懲罰的（罕見詞）
sentences: In older legal systems, certain acts were considered impunible under diplomatic immunity. 在舊的法律體系中，某些行為在外交豁免下被認為是不可懲罰的。
The impunible status of the official was widely criticized by the public. 官員不受懲罰的地位受到公眾的廣泛批評。
The warlord ruled impunibly for decades due to foreign protection. 這個軍閥在外國保護下不受懲罰地統治了數十年。
Crimes were committed impunibly in areas with no effective law enforcement. 在沒有有效執法的地區，犯罪不受懲罰地發生。

meaning: 免受懲罰或報復；免受任何報復或行動、行為等造成的傷害性後果的保障。
sentences: The term impunity refers to freedom from punishment or retribution; security from any reprisal or injurious consequences of an action, behaviour etc. 有罪不罰一詞是指免受懲罰或報復；免受任何行動、行為等報復或傷害性後果的保障。
The lesson introduced impunity as a useful term to understand. 本課介紹了有罪不罰這個術語，這是一個值得理解的有用術語。

meaning: 不貞潔的；淫穢的（不符合或不遵守某些性道德體系）
sentences: He was thinking impure thoughts involving a girl from school. 他正在思考與學校裡的一個女孩有關的不純潔的想法。
The report describes the result as impure. 報告稱結果不純。
The metal was impurely refined, containing traces of other elements. 這種金屬被不純地精煉，含有其他元素的痕跡。
He approached the sacred ritual impurely, without proper preparation. 他在沒有適當準備的情況下不純潔地參加了神聖儀式。

meaning: 不純潔；不純（impurity 的同義詞）
sentences: The impureness of the water sample indicated contamination. 水樣的不純表明受到了污染。
His impureness of motive made others question his integrity. 他動機的不純潔使他人質疑他的誠信。

meaning: 反清教徒的人；反對清教主義者（歷史術語）
sentences: The impuritan celebrated festivities that the Puritans had banned. 反清教徒者慶祝了清教徒禁止的節日。
As an impuritan, he rejected the strict moral codes of his era. 作為反清教主義者，他拒絕了那個時代嚴格的道德規範。

meaning: 反清教主義；對清教道德的反對（歷史術語）
sentences: Impuritanism was a cultural reaction against the strictness of Puritan rule. 反清教主義是對清教徒統治嚴苛性的文化反應。
Historians study impuritanism as a social phenomenon of the 17th century. 歷史學家研究反清教主義作為17世紀的社會現象。
The chemist filtered out the impurity before proceeding with the experiment. 化學家在繼續實驗之前過濾掉了雜質。
Moral impurity was considered a grave offense in ancient religious texts. 在古代宗教文本中，道德上的不純潔被視為嚴重的冒犯。

meaning: 可歸責性；可歸因性（法律/倫理術語）
sentences: The imputability of the accident to negligence was established in court. 事故歸因於疏忽的可歸責性在法庭上得到了確認。
Philosophers debate the imputability of actions performed under coercion. 哲學家討論在脅迫下採取行動的可歸責性。

meaning: 可歸責的；可歸因的（法律/倫理術語）
sentences: The losses were imputable to the manager's poor decisions. 這些損失可歸因於經理糟糕的決策。
Under the law, the crime was imputable to both defendants equally. 根據法律，這項罪行可以平等地歸責於兩名被告。

meaning: 可歸責性；可歸因性（罕見詞）
sentences: The imputableness of the error to human factors was widely accepted. 將錯誤歸因於人為因素的可歸責性被廣泛接受。
Legal scholars debated the imputableness of the act given the circumstances. 法律學者就考慮到情況下該行為的可歸責性進行了辯論。

meaning: 可歸責地；以可歸因的方式
sentences: The failure was imputably caused by a lack of proper supervision. 這次失敗可歸責地是由於缺乏適當的監督造成的。
She was imputably responsible for the breach of contract, the court ruled. 法院裁定，她對違約可歸責地負有責任。

meaning: 歸因；歸罪；歸咎（將某事歸因於某人）
sentences: The imputation of blame to the entire team was unfair to those who worked hard. 將責任歸咎於整個團隊對那些努力工作的人是不公平的。
The imputation of dishonesty damaged his reputation significantly. 誠信方面的歸罪嚴重損害了他的聲譽。

meaning: 歸因的；被歸咎的（神學術語）
sentences: In Christian theology, imputative righteousness refers to righteousness credited to sinners through faith. 在基督教神學中，歸因義是指通過信心歸算給罪人的義。
The imputative nature of the verdict was debated by legal scholars. 判決的歸因性質被法律學者所討論。

meaning: 通過歸因；以歸罪的方式
sentences: In religious discourse, grace is imputatively bestowed on believers. 在宗教論述中，恩典被歸因地賜予信徒。
He was imputatively blamed even though he had no direct involvement. 儘管他沒有直接參與，他還是被歸因地責備了。

meaning: 歸因的傾向；歸咎性（罕見詞）
sentences: His imputativeness made him quick to assign blame without proper investigation. 他的歸咎傾向使他在沒有適當調查的情況下迅速分配責任。
The imputativeness of the accusation was its most damaging quality. 指控的歸咎性是其最具破壞力的特質。

meaning: 歸咎；歸因；將（某事）歸於（某人）
sentences: It would be wrong to impute malicious intent without any evidence. 在沒有任何證據的情況下歸咎於惡意是錯誤的。
The professor tried to impute the origin of the manuscript to a specific author. 教授試圖將這份手稿的起源歸因於特定的作者。
The crime was imputedly carried out by someone with inside knowledge. 這項犯罪被歸因地由某個有內部知識的人實施。
The manuscript was imputedly written in the 15th century, though proof is lacking. 這份手稿被歸因地寫於15世紀，儘管缺乏證明。
As an imputer of blame, he was often resented by his colleagues. 作為一個歸咎者，他常常被同事所怨恨。
The historical imputer of the theory was later proved wrong. 最初歸因這個理論的人後來被證明是錯誤的。

meaning: 不腐性；不易腐爛的狀態
sentences: The imputrescence of amber has made it valuable for preserving ancient specimens. 琥珀的不腐性使其在保存古老標本方面具有價值。
Scientists studied the imputrescence of the mummy's remains. 科學家研究了木乃伊遺骸的不腐性。

meaning: 不腐性；不易腐爛的特質
sentences: The imputrescibility of certain plastics makes them an environmental concern. 某些塑料的不腐性使其成為環境問題。
Wood preservatives enhance imputrescibility, extending the lifespan of timber structures. 木材防腐劑增強了不腐性，延長了木結構的使用壽命。
The archaeologist was amazed by the imputrescible condition of the ancient cloth. 考古學家對這塊古布不腐爛的狀況感到驚訝。
Certain synthetic materials are imputrescible, which complicates waste disposal. 某些合成材料是不腐爛的，這使廢物處理複雜化。

meaning: 不腐敗的；不腐爛的（罕見詞）
sentences: The imputrid wood was examined by scientists studying natural preservation. 科學家對不腐爛的木材進行了研究，研究自然保存。
Medieval scholars believed certain sacred objects were imputrid by divine protection. 中世紀學者相信某些神聖物品受到神聖保護而不腐爛。

meaning: 頑皮的；惡作劇的；像小惡魔的（口語）
sentences: The impy kitten knocked everything off the table for fun. 頑皮的小貓為了好玩把桌上所有東西都推倒了。
Her impy grin told everyone she was up to something mischievous. 她頑皮的笑容告訴所有人她正在打什麼壞主意。

meaning: 走開；滾開（源自阿拉伯語，英國殖民時期俚語）
sentences: The soldier shouted 'imshi!' to drive away the crowd. 士兵大喊「走開！」驅散人群。
Imshi was a common expression used in British colonial settings in the Middle East. 「imshi」是英國殖民時期在中東常用的表達。

meaning: 與聲波或聲音圖像有關的（技術術語）
sentences: The imsonic technology allowed for high-resolution imaging using sound. 聲波圖像技術允許使用聲音進行高解析度成像。
Researchers developed an imsonic device for medical diagnostics. 研究人員開發了一種用於醫學診斷的聲波圖像裝置。
The pig was slow-cooked in an imu for the traditional luau feast. 豬肉在地下烤爐中慢烤，用於傳統的露天宴會。
Building an imu is a skill passed down through generations in Hawaiian culture. 建造地下烤爐是夏威夷文化中代代相傳的技能。
My keys are in my bag; I'll get them for you. 我的鑰匙在包包裡，我這就拿給妳。
He lives in a small apartment in the city center. 他住在市中心的一間小公寓裡。

meaning: 看到自己寫的短篇小說發表在當地的
sentences: "in newspaper" is useful when you want to sound natural in conversation. 當你想讓對話更自然時，「in newspaper」非常好用。
Our teacher asked us to memorize "in newspaper" for next week. 老師要我們把「in newspaper」背起來，下週要用。
His inability to speak English made it hard for him to find a job. 他不會說英語使他很難找到工作。
The company's inability to adapt to changes led to its failure. 公司無法適應變化導致了其失敗。

meaning: 難以接近的；令人望而生畏的（源自法語）
sentences: The professor's inabordable demeanor discouraged students from asking questions. 教授令人望而生畏的態度阻止了學生提問。
The inabordable cliffs made the castle virtually impossible to storm. 難以接近的懸崖使得這座城堡幾乎無法強攻。

meaning: 無節制；放縱（abstinence 的反義詞）
sentences: His inabstinence from rich foods led to significant health problems. 他對豐盛食物的無節制導致了嚴重的健康問題。
The doctor warned that inabstinence from alcohol was putting his liver at risk. 醫生警告說，對酒精的無節制使他的肝臟處於危險之中。
The inaccentuated syllables in the poem created a flat, monotone effect. 詩中未被強調的音節造成了平淡、單調的效果。
In her inaccentuated speech, all words seemed to carry equal weight. 在她未強調的說話中，所有詞似乎都具有同等的重量。

meaning: 缺乏重音；無強調（語言學術語）
sentences: The inaccentuation in his delivery made it difficult to identify key points. 他演說中的缺乏重音使人難以辨識關鍵點。
Linguists studied inaccentuation as a feature of certain dialects. 語言學家研究缺乏重音作為某些方言的特徵。

meaning: 不可接受的（unacceptable 的變體）
sentences: The committee found the proposed terms inacceptable and rejected them. 委員會認為提議的條款不可接受並予以拒絕。
Such inacceptable behavior cannot be tolerated in a professional setting. 這種不可接受的行為在專業環境中是不能被容忍的。

meaning: 無法進入性；難以接近性
sentences: The inaccessibility of the mountain peak made the climb extremely challenging. 山頂的無法進入性使攀登極具挑戰性。
The inaccessibility of the records frustrated historians trying to research the era. 記錄的難以接近性讓試圖研究那個時代的歷史學家感到沮喪。

meaning: 無法訪問；遙不可及；不方便。
sentences: physically inaccessible 物理上无法接近
remote and inaccessible 远程且无法访问

meaning: 無法接近的狀態；難以到達性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inaccessibleness of the archive made original research nearly impossible. 檔案館的難以接近性使原始研究幾乎不可能。
Its inaccessibleness gave the island a sense of wild, untouched beauty. 難以到達的特性賦予了這座島嶼野性、未受觸碰的美感。

meaning: 以無法接近的方式；難以到達地
sentences: The treasure was inaccessibly hidden deep within the mountain cave. 寶藏被難以接近地藏在山洞深處。
The information was stored inaccessibly in outdated formats. 這些信息被以過時格式難以接近地儲存著。
The inaccordance between the report and the data raised concerns. 報告與數據之間的不一致引起了關注。
Scholars noted the inaccordance of the document with historical records. 學者們注意到該文件與歷史記錄的不符合。

meaning: 不一致；不和諧（inaccordance 的舊式變體）
sentences: The inaccordancy in the scientific findings prompted further investigation. 科學發現中的不一致促使了進一步調查。
There was an obvious inaccordancy between his words and his actions. 他的言語和行動之間存在明顯的不和諧。
His testimony was inaccordant with the physical evidence found at the scene. 他的證詞與現場找到的物證不一致。
The two theories are inaccordant and cannot both be true simultaneously. 這兩個理論是不一致的，不能同時為真。
The witnesses spoke inaccordantly, making the investigation more complex. 證人們說法不一致，使調查更加複雜。
He acted inaccordantly with the established protocols, causing confusion. 他的行為與既定協議不一致，造成了混亂。
The report contained several inaccuracies that needed to be corrected. 報告包含幾個需要更正的不準確之處。
Even small inaccuracies in calculations can lead to significant errors in engineering. 即使是計算中的小錯誤也可能在工程中導致重大誤差。
Something described as inaccurate is mistaken or incorrect; not accurate. 被描述為不準確的事物是錯誤的或不正確的；不準確。
The word inaccurate is often used to describe a person, thing, or situation. 不準確這個詞經常被用來描述一個人、一件事或情況。
The map was inaccurately drawn, leading travelers astray. 地圖被不準確地繪製，使旅行者迷路。
She inaccurately recalled the date of the meeting, causing a scheduling conflict. 她不正確地回憶了會議日期，導致了日程衝突。

meaning: 不準確的性質；不精確性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inaccurateness of the measurements was later discovered in quality control. 測量的不準確性後來在質量控制中被發現。
Critics pointed to the inaccurateness of the historical novel's depiction. 批評者指出了歷史小說描述的不準確性。

meaning: 屬於蜘蛛蟹科（Inachidae）的；蜘蛛蟹（生物學術語）
sentences: The inachid was identified by its long, spindly legs characteristic of spider crabs. 這種蜘蛛蟹由其長而細的蜘蛛蟹特徵腿來辨識。
Biologists studied the inachid's behavior in its natural tidal habitat. 生物學家研究了這種蜘蛛蟹在其自然潮汐棲息地中的行為。

meaning: 蜘蛛蟹科（甲殼類動物分類）
sentences: Members of the family Inachidae are commonly known as spider crabs. 蜘蛛蟹科的成員通常被稱為蜘蛛蟹。
The Inachidae are found in a variety of marine environments worldwide. 蜘蛛蟹科分布在全球各種海洋環境中。

meaning: 類似蜘蛛蟹的；蜘蛛蟹型的（生物學術語）
sentences: The inachoid body plan is characterized by an elongated carapace and thin legs. 蜘蛛蟹型的體型特徵是細長的甲殼和細腿。
Researchers found an inachoid species previously unknown to science. 研究人員發現了一種科學上以前未知的蜘蛛蟹型物種。

meaning: 希臘神話中的河神；蜘蛛蟹屬的屬名
sentences: Inachus was a river god in Greek mythology and the father of Io. 伊納科斯是希臘神話中的河神，也是伊娥的父親。
The genus Inachus includes several species of spider crabs found in European waters. 伊納科斯屬包括在歐洲水域發現的幾種蜘蛛蟹。
Her inacquaintance with local customs led to several misunderstandings. 她對當地習俗的不熟悉導致了幾次誤解。
The diplomat's inacquaintance with the language was a significant disadvantage. 外交官對這門語言的不熟悉是一個重大不利因素。

meaning: 不順從的；不同意的（罕見詞）
sentences: The inacquiescent employee refused to accept the new working conditions. 不順從的員工拒絕接受新的工作條件。
His inacquiescent attitude made him difficult to manage. 他不順從的態度使他難以管理。

meaning: 不產生化學反應的（光線）；非活性化學光的（攝影術語）
sentences: Inactinic light is used in darkrooms where photosensitive materials are handled. 非活性化學光燈在處理感光材料的暗室中使用。
The inactinic lamp prevented accidental exposure of the photographic plates. 非活性化學光燈防止了感光板的意外曝光。
The term inaction refers to Lack of action or activity or labor. 「不作為」一詞是指缺乏行動、活動或勞動。
We learned the meaning of inaction during today's lesson. 在今天的課程中我們學到了不作為的意義。

meaning: 主張不作為者；支持無為而治者（罕見詞）
sentences: The political inactionist argued that government intervention caused more harm than good. 政治不作為主張者認為政府干預造成的傷害多於利益。
As an inactionist, she believed that the situation would resolve itself without interference. 作為主張不作為者，她相信情況會在不干預的情況下自行解決。
Scientists worked to inactivate the virus before using it in the vaccine. 科學家在將病毒用於疫苗之前努力使其失活。
Heating the enzyme will inactivate it, stopping the chemical reaction. 加熱酶會使其失活，從而停止化學反應。

meaning: 失活；去活化；使無效的過程
sentences: The inactivation of the pathogen was confirmed through laboratory testing. 病原體的失活通過實驗室測試得到了確認。
Inactivation of certain enzymes by temperature changes is a well-known phenomenon. 某些酶因溫度變化而失活是一個眾所周知的現象。
The volcano has been inactive for over a century. 這座火山已經沈寂了一個多世紀。
The account has been inactive for several months. 該帳戶已經停用幾個月了。
He inactively waited for opportunities instead of seeking them out. 他不積極地等待機會，而不是主動尋找它們。
The volcano has been inactively dormant for three centuries. 這座火山已經不活躍地休眠了三個世紀。

meaning: 不活躍；不積極；懈怠（罕見詞）
sentences: The inactiveness of the government during the crisis was heavily criticized. 政府在危機期間的不積極受到了嚴厲批評。
His inactiveness during the project contributed to missed deadlines. 他在專案中的懈怠導致了截止日期的延誤。

meaning: 不活躍的品質；懶惰;被動性。
sentences: The term inactivity refers to the quality of being inactive; idleness; passiveness. 不活動這個字是指不活動的品質。懶惰;被動性。
The lesson introduced inactivity as a useful term to understand. 本課程介紹了「不活動」是一個需要理解的有用術語。

meaning: 使無法運作；阻止啟動（罕見詞）
sentences: The power failure inactuated all the machines in the facility. 停電使設施中的所有機器無法運作。
A design flaw inactuated the safety mechanism during testing. 設計缺陷在測試期間阻止了安全機制的啟動。

meaning: 無法啟動；阻止運作的狀態（罕見詞）
sentences: The inactuation of the failsafe system was traced to a software bug. 故障安全系統的無法啟動被追溯到軟件錯誤。
Inactuation of the planned reforms left many citizens frustrated. 計劃改革的無法啟動使許多公民感到沮喪。
The species' inadaptability to climate change threatens its survival. 該物種對氣候變化的不適應性威脅到其生存。
His inadaptability to new technology slowed the team's progress. 他對新技術的無法適應減慢了團隊的進度。

meaning: 不能適應的；無法調整的
sentences: The old software was inadaptable to the new operating system. 舊軟件無法適應新操作系統。
He proved inadaptable in the rapidly changing work environment. 他在快速變化的工作環境中被證明無法適應。
The inadaptation of the immigrants to urban life was a temporary challenge. 移民對城市生活的不適應是一個暫時的挑戰。
Evolutionary inadaptation can lead to extinction over time. 進化上的不適應會隨著時間導致滅絕。

meaning: 不適應的；缺乏適應性的
sentences: His inadaptive behavior made it difficult to integrate into the new team. 他不適應的行為使融入新團隊變得困難。
Inadaptive responses to stress can lead to long-term mental health problems. 對壓力的不適應性反應可能導致長期的心理健康問題。
She was inadept at handling confrontation and would often avoid conflict. 她不擅長處理對抗，常常迴避衝突。
As an inadept cook, he relied on simple recipes to get by. 作為一個不擅長烹飪的人，他依賴簡單的食譜應付。
The inadequacy of the public transport system frustrated daily commuters. 公共交通系統的不足使日常通勤者感到沮喪。
Feelings of inadequacy can undermine a person's confidence. 不足感可能削弱一個人的自信心。

meaning: 不充分；缺乏所需的品質或數量；不足以達到某個目的。
sentences: inadequate resources 資源不足
inadequate representation 代表性不足
The workers were inadequately trained for the dangerous task. 工人們對這項危險任務的培訓不充分。
The shelter was inadequately stocked with supplies before the storm hit. 在暴風雨來臨之前，避難所的物資補充不充分。

meaning: 不充分的性質；不足性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inadequateness of the solution was clear once the problem recurred. 一旦問題再次出現，解決方案的不足性就很明顯了。
Critics highlighted the inadequateness of the government's response. 批評者強調了政府回應的不充分性。

meaning: 不相稱；不匹配（哲學術語）
sentences: The inadequation between theory and practice was a central concern. 理論與實踐之間的不相稱是核心關注點。
Philosophers speak of inadequation when concepts fail to match reality. 當概念未能與現實相匹配時，哲學家們談到不相稱。

meaning: 不充分的；不相稱的（哲學術語）
sentences: The inadequative representation of complex reality was a philosophical problem. 對複雜現實的不充分表達是一個哲學問題。
His inadequative analysis of the data led to faulty conclusions. 他對數據的不充分分析導致了錯誤的結論。

meaning: 不充分地；以不相稱的方式（哲學術語）
sentences: The concept was inadequatively defined, causing ongoing confusion. 這個概念被不充分地定義，持續造成混亂。
He inadequatively addressed the core issue in his response. 他在回應中不充分地處理了核心問題。
The inadherent coating peeled away after only a few weeks. 不附著的塗層在幾周後就脫落了。
Inadherent particles in the mixture required additional binders. 混合物中不附著的顆粒需要額外的黏合劑。
The inadhesion of the new material was identified as the cause of the failure. 新材料的不黏附被確定為失敗的原因。
Engineers worked to solve the problem of inadhesion in the composite layers. 工程師致力於解決複合層中不黏附的問題。
The inadhesive surface was intentionally designed for easy removal. 不黏的表面被有意設計為易於去除。
An inadhesive film is often used in packaging for products that must remain dry. 不黏薄膜常用於包裝必須保持乾燥的產品。
The inadjustability of the equipment limited its use in varied environments. 設備的不可調整性限制了其在不同環境中的使用。
The inadjustability of the tax brackets caused financial hardship for some groups. 稅級的不可調整性給某些群體造成了財務困難。

meaning: 不可調整的；無法調節的
sentences: The inadjustable thermostat frustrated the building's occupants during extreme weather. 不可調整的恆溫器在極端天氣中令建築居民感到沮喪。
An inadjustable salary structure can make it hard to attract top talent. 不可調整的薪資結構可能使吸引頂尖人才變得困難。

meaning: 不可接受性；不被允許；不得采信（法律術語）
sentences: The judge ruled on the inadmissibility of the evidence due to procedural flaws. 法官因程序缺陷裁定該證據的不被允許。
Questions of inadmissibility were central to the pretrial hearings. 不被允許的問題是審前聆訊的核心。

meaning: 不可接受的；不得采信的（法律術語）
sentences: The confession was ruled inadmissible because it was obtained under duress. 供詞被裁定不得采信，因為它是在脅迫下取得的。
Hearsay evidence is typically inadmissible in criminal trials. 傳聞證據在刑事審判中通常是不得采信的。

meaning: 不可接受地；以不得采信的方式
sentences: The evidence was inadmissibly obtained, rendering the case vulnerable. 證據被不可接受地取得，使案件脆弱。
His remarks were inadmissibly prejudicial and were struck from the record. 他的言論被不可接受地帶有偏見，並從記錄中刪除了。

meaning: 缺乏冒險精神的；保守的
sentences: The inadventurous traveler preferred familiar destinations over exotic ones. 缺乏冒險精神的旅行者偏愛熟悉的目的地而非異國的地方。
His inadventurous approach to investing kept him safe but limited his gains. 他對投資缺乏冒險精神的方式使他保持安全，但限制了他的收益。

meaning: 疏忽；不注意；非故意的失誤
sentences: The error was the result of inadvertence rather than intentional deception. 錯誤是疏忽的結果，而非故意欺騙。
Through inadvertence, she sent the email to the wrong recipient. 由於疏忽，她將電子郵件發送給了錯誤的收件人。

meaning: 疏忽；不注意（inadvertence 的變體）
sentences: His inadvertency in checking the contract led to unforeseen complications. 他在檢查合約時的疏忽導致了意想不到的複雜情況。
The accident was attributed to inadvertency rather than any malice. 事故被歸因於疏忽而非任何惡意。

meaning: 疏忽的；無意的；非故意的
sentences: The scientist made an inadvertent error in the experiment, affecting the results. 科學家在實驗中犯了一個無意的錯誤，影響了結果。
His inadvertent disclosure of the plan caused major disruptions. 他無意中洩露計劃造成了重大混亂。

meaning: 無意地；疏忽地；不知不覺地
sentences: She inadvertently left her keys at the office when she went home. 她在回家時無意地把鑰匙留在了辦公室。
The journalist inadvertently revealed his source during the interview. 記者在採訪中無意地透露了他的消息來源。
The inadvisability of the plan was pointed out by senior management. 高層管理人員指出了該計劃的不明智之處。
The inadvisability of hiking alone in the mountains cannot be overstated. 獨自在山中遠足的不可取性怎麼說都不為過。

meaning: 不明智的；不可取的；不宜的
sentences: It is inadvisable to make major decisions when you're emotionally upset. 在情緒低落時做重大決定是不明智的。
The doctor considered it inadvisable for the patient to travel. 醫生認為患者旅行是不可取的。

meaning: 不明智的性質；不可取性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inadvisableness of the venture was clear from the outset. 這次冒險的不可取性從一開始就很明顯。
Her advisor explained the inadvisableness of withdrawing from the program early. 她的顧問解釋了提前退出計劃的不明智性。

meaning: 不明智地；魯莽地；輕率地
sentences: He inadvisedly quit his job without having any savings to fall back on. 他魯莽地辭去了工作，卻沒有任何積蓄可以依靠。
She inadvisedly agreed to the terms without consulting a lawyer. 她輕率地在沒有諮詢律師的情況下同意了這些條款。

meaning: 不美的；缺乏美感的（anaesthetic 的反義詞形式，罕見用法）
sentences: The inaesthetic design of the building was widely criticized by architects. 建築物不美的設計受到了建築師的廣泛批評。
His inaesthetic choice of colors for the poster disappointed the art director. 他對海報顏色缺乏美感的選擇讓藝術總監失望了。

meaning: 不親切；不易接近（affability 的反義詞）
sentences: His inaffability in the workplace made him difficult to approach with problems. 他在工作場所的不親切使人難以向他提出問題。
The inaffability of the new manager created tension among the staff. 新經理的不易接近在員工中造成了緊張氣氛。

meaning: 不親切的；不易接近的（affable 的反義詞）
sentences: The inaffable professor rarely engaged with students outside of lectures. 不易接近的教授很少在課堂外與學生互動。
His inaffable manner put off potential clients during the initial meeting. 他不親切的態度在初次會面時讓潛在客戶感到不快。

meaning: 無做作；自然；真實（罕見詞）
sentences: The actor's inaffectation on screen made his performances feel completely real. 演員在屏幕上的無做作使他的表演感覺完全真實。
Her inaffectation in social settings was one of her most charming qualities. 她在社交場合的自然是她最迷人的品質之一。

meaning: 不可凝集性（醫學/生物學術語）
sentences: The inagglutinability of certain blood types is important in transfusion medicine. 某些血型的不可凝集性在輸血醫學中很重要。
Researchers studied the inagglutinability of the new bacterial strain. 研究人員研究了新細菌菌株的不可凝集性。

meaning: 不可凝集的；不會凝結的（生物學術語）
sentences: The inagglutinable nature of the sample complicated the diagnostic process. 樣本不可凝集的性質使診斷過程複雜化了。
Scientists developed a test to identify inagglutinable blood cell variants. 科學家開發了一種測試來識別不可凝集的血細胞變體。

meaning: 非侵略性的；不具攻擊性的
sentences: The country pursued an inaggressive foreign policy, focusing on diplomacy. 這個國家奉行非侵略性的外交政策，專注於外交。
The inaggressive dog was popular with children at the park. 不具攻擊性的狗在公園裡很受孩子們歡迎。
The inagile robot struggled to navigate the cluttered workspace. 不敏捷的機器人在雜亂的工作空間中艱難前行。
As he aged, he became more inagile but compensated with experience. 隨著年齡增長，他變得更不敏捷，但以經驗加以彌補。

meaning: 無法得到幫助的；無可救藥的（罕見詞）
sentences: The inaidable situation left all parties at a loss for solutions. 無法得到幫助的情況讓各方都束手無策。
He considered the political crisis inaidable by conventional means. 他認為這場政治危機無法通過常規手段解決。
The inaja palm is native to the Amazon region and produces edible fruits. 印阿加棕櫚樹是亞馬遜地區的原生植物，能產出可食用的果實。
Indigenous communities have long used the inaja for food and building materials. 原住民社群長期以來使用印阿加棕櫚樹作為食物和建築材料。

meaning: 缺乏積極性；不熱衷（alacrity 的反義詞）
sentences: His inalacrity in responding to urgent requests was a management concern. 他在回應緊急請求時缺乏積極性是管理層的擔憂。
Her inalacrity toward the project revealed her disinterest in the assignment. 她對專案缺乏熱衷揭示了她對任務的不感興趣。

meaning: 不可讓與性；不可剝奪性（法律術語）
sentences: The inalienability of human rights is enshrined in international law. 人權的不可剝奪性被載入國際法。
Legal scholars debate the inalienability of certain property rights. 法律學者討論某些財產權的不可讓與性。

meaning: 不可讓與的；不可剝奪的（法律術語）
sentences: The right to a fair trial is considered inalienable in democratic societies. 在民主社會中，公正審判的權利被視為不可剝奪的。
The inalienable rights of citizens are protected by the constitution. 公民的不可剝奪權利受到憲法的保護。

meaning: 不可讓與性；不可剝奪性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inalienableness of human dignity is a cornerstone of human rights theory. 人的尊嚴的不可剝奪性是人權理論的基石。
The court affirmed the inalienableness of the freedom of conscience. 法院確認了良心自由的不可剝奪性。

meaning: 不可讓與地；不可剝奪地
sentences: All people are inalienably endowed with certain basic rights. 所有人都不可剝奪地被賦予某些基本權利。
These freedoms are inalienably protected under the constitution. 這些自由在憲法下被不可剝奪地保護著。

meaning: 無營養的；不提供養分的（罕見詞）
sentences: The inalimental snack provided no real sustenance for the hungry workers. 無營養的零食對飢餓的工人沒有提供任何真正的養分。
Critics argued the program was inalimental — addressing symptoms but not root causes. 批評者認為該計劃是無養分的——只解決症狀而不解決根本原因。

meaning: 不可改變性；不可更改性
sentences: The inalterability of the laws of physics is a foundational assumption in science. 物理定律的不可改變性是科學中的基本假設。
Legal scholars debated the inalterability of the constitution's core provisions. 法律學者討論了憲法核心條款的不可更改性。

meaning: 不可改變的；不可更改的
sentences: The judge declared the terms of the agreement inalterable once signed. 法官宣布協議的條款一旦簽署就不可更改。
Some believe the moral laws of the universe are inalterable. 有些人相信宇宙的道德法則是不可改變的。

meaning: 不可改變的性質；不可更改性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inalterableness of genetic traits was once believed to be absolute. 基因特徵的不可改變性曾被認為是絕對的。
Philosophers explored the inalterableness of moral truths across cultures. 哲學家探索了跨文化道德真理的不可改變性。

meaning: 不可改變地；不可更改地
sentences: The decision was inalterably made, with no room for reconsideration. 這個決定已不可更改地做出，沒有重新考慮的餘地。
Her opinion on the matter was inalterably fixed despite the new evidence. 儘管有新的證據，她對這件事的看法也不可改變地固定了。

meaning: 不可失去性；不可喪失性（神學術語）
sentences: Theologians argued about the inamissibility of grace once bestowed by God. 神學家們爭論著一旦上帝賜予恩典後的不可失去性。
The inamissibility of salvation was a central doctrine in certain theological traditions. 救恩的不可失去性是某些神學傳統中的核心教義。

meaning: 不可失去的；不可喪失的（神學術語）
sentences: Some Christian denominations believe in inamissible grace, once saved always saved. 一些基督教派別相信不可失去的恩典——一旦得救便永遠得救。
The inamissible nature of certain natural rights was debated by philosophers. 某些自然權利的不可失去性被哲學家們所討論。

meaning: 不可失去性；不可喪失性（罕見詞）
sentences: The inamissibleness of certain rights was a key argument in the legal case. 某些權利的不可失去性是法律案件中的關鍵論點。
Theologians wrote extensively on the inamissibleness of divine grace. 神學家廣泛撰寫了關於神聖恩典的不可失去性。

meaning: 被愛慕的女性；情人；戀人（義大利語借詞）
sentences: He wrote love poems to his inamorata every day for a year. 他每天為他的情人寫愛情詩，持續了一年。
The opera told the story of a nobleman and his inamorata from a rival family. 這部歌劇講述了一位貴族和他來自敵對家族的情人的故事。
